rootCause: Race condition bug in NMAgent (Azure networking service) causes ACI to resolve the service endpoint's public IP instead of private IP. Stale records in DnsForwarder are not cleaned up, causing persistent incorrect DNS resolution for Private Link endpoints.
solution: 1. Query Kusto SubscriptionDeployments with containerGroup resourceURI to get node and clusterId. 2. Query ClusterHealth with clusterId to get infra VM subscription ID from manifest. 3. Create IcM to Cloudnet\DNS Recursive team with infra VM subscription ID and VM name. 4. Link IcM to parent IcM 226910502. 5. Ask Cloudnet team to clean up stale DnsForwarder records. New deployments should resume normal functionality after cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Private Link or Private DNS are resolving to azure resource's Public IP instead of private IP

symptom: ACI Spot container group enters 'Waiting' state after having been in 'Running' state. All containers in the container group are stuck waiting for a new node to be assigned, with no recovery.
rootCause: The underlying Spot VM hosting the container group was evicted due to Azure capacity adjustments. Spot VM evictions can happen at any time. The container group waits for relocation to a new node within the same or different cluster.
solution: This is expected behavior for Spot containers. Use the Spot ContainerGroup Evictions Kusto query (atlaslogscp.eastus/telemetry) with CRP ApiQosEvent_nonGet to confirm evictions (FabricCallback.OnVMScaleSetVMsEvicted.POST), track jump count and jump durations. See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-aci-spot-containers-evictions-kusto.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Waiting State Due To Evictions

symptom: ACStor Data Integrity Scrubber failure; log messages indicating 'volume replicas or children checksums go out of sync or fail to match'; core-agent logs show 'Replicas of volume <id> are out of sync for segments [...]'
rootCause: ACStor background scrubber detects replica checksums don't match; if out-of-sync spans all replicas (no quorum) auto-rebuild cannot proceed; may be triggered when IOs modify blocks during scrub window
solution: Bring up the application pod and let it run for several hours — scrubber auto-mitigates by partial rebuild from healthy replicas (estimate: ~8min/GB). Monitor core-agent logs for '...completed up to N segment'. If all replicas out of sync (no quorum for rebuild), use volume snapshot to recover the volume
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Data Integrity Scrubber Failure

symptom: Private AKS cluster nodes cannot resolve API server private FQDN when custom DNS server is configured in the VNet (on Azure or on-prem)
rootCause: Custom DNS server cannot resolve A records under Azure private DNS zone. The DNS queries need to be forwarded to Azure default DNS server (168.63.129.16). Additionally, the private DNS zone must have a virtual network link to the DNS server VNet.
solution: 1) For Azure-based DNS server: link the private DNS zone to both the custom DNS server VNet and AKS VNet (AKS VNet is auto-linked). Configure DNS forwarding to 168.63.129.16. 2) For on-prem DNS server: set up an Azure VM as DNS forwarder, link private DNS zone to that VM VNet. On-prem DNS forwards to Azure VM, which forwards to 168.63.129.16. Key: ensure virtual network link exists between private DNS zone and DNS server VNet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/How Tos/Networking/Setup private AKS with custom DNS server

symptom: Azure portal cannot list ACR container images when deploying AKS 'Starter Application' — CORS error 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin header not present' and HTTP 400 from registry endpoint. Only affects users with large Azure AD group memberships.
rootCause: OAuth2 bearer tokens for users with many Azure AD group memberships exceed 8192 bytes (max HTTP header size accepted by ACR endpoints). The oversized token causes the registry to reject the request without returning CORS headers, so the browser blocks the response. Users below the 'overage' threshold but with many groups are specifically affected.
solution: 1) Permanent fix deployed by PG: Azure portal updated to remove Authorization header from browser HTTP request, relying solely on auth token in request body for CORS authorization. 2) Temporary workaround: affected users can use Azure CLI or ARM templates for deployments (no CORS involved). 3) PG can provide temporary portal 'flight' link for affected users as interim workaround. 4) Verify by capturing HAR logs and browser console output to confirm CORS errors and HTTP 400.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/ACR CORS Policy Blocked Portal Issue

symptom: Client requests are not being routed to the same pod consistently across consecutive requests in an AKS Kubernetes service
rootCause: Kubernetes Service sessionAffinity is set to None by default, so requests are distributed across pods without stickiness.
solution: Set service.spec.sessionAffinity to ClientIP and optionally configure service.spec.sessionAffinityConfig.clientIP.timeoutSeconds (default 10800 = 3 hours). Example: spec.sessionAffinity: ClientIP, spec.sessionAffinityConfig.clientIP.timeoutSeconds: 900
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/How Tos/Networking/Configuring session stickiness with services

symptom: AKS nodes cannot resolve API server private FQDN in a private AKS cluster with custom DNS server
rootCause: Custom DNS server cannot resolve A records under Azure private DNS zone. DNS queries not forwarded to Azure default DNS (168.63.129.16), or virtual network link between custom DNS VNet and private DNS zone is missing.
solution: For Azure-hosted custom DNS: 1) Link private DNS zone to custom DNS server VNet. 2) Forward queries to 168.63.129.16. For on-prem DNS: 1) Set up Azure VM as forwarder. 2) Link private DNS zone to VM VNet. 3) On-prem forwards to Azure VM, VM forwards to 168.63.129.16. AKS VNet auto-linked but custom DNS VNet must also be linked.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/How Tos/Networking/Setup private AKS with custom DNS server

symptom: AKS virtual node 创建失败: error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table
rootCause: ACI (virtual node) 子网不支持 User-Defined Routing (UDR)。aci-connector 会校验并阻止使用关联了路由表的子网进行委派
solution: 从 ACI virtual node 使用的子网中移除 User-Defined Routing (路由表)。ACI 服务不支持子网上的 UDR
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 404 Not Found，请求路径与后端应用期望路径不匹配
rootCause: Application Gateway 将请求转发到后端时使用了客户端原始路径，但后端应用期望在不同的路径上接收请求
solution: 在 ingress resource 上添加 appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-path-prefix 注解，将外部请求路径重写为后端应用实际期望的路径。也可修改应用配置使其在请求的路径上监听，或修改客户端请求 URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 404 Not Found

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 502 Bad Gateway，特别是在 K8s 工作负载滚动更新期间频繁出现
rootCause: Application Gateway 后端池中没有健康的后端成员。在 K8s rolling update 期间旧 Pod 已终止但新 Pod 尚未就绪时，会短暂出现无可用后端的情况
solution: 1. 检查 AppGW Backend Health 确认后端健康状态。2. 如果 502 在 rolling update 期间频发，按照 AGIC 'Minimizing Downtime During Deployments' 文档配置 preStop hook、readinessProbe 和 minReadySeconds 来减少部署期间的停机时间
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 502 Bad Gateway

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout，响应时间与配置的 request-timeout 一致
rootCause: Application Gateway 的 request timeout（默认 30s）过短，后端应用处理请求耗时超过该超时值
solution: 在 ingress resource 上设置 appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/request-timeout 注解增大超时值（单位秒）。通过 kubectl describe ingress 确认当前值，在 Azure Portal AppGW Backend Settings 中验证生效。如果 504 时间与 timeout 不一致，需排查后端应用本身问题
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 504 Gateway Timeout

symptom: AGIC pod 日志显示 ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError: Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway，伴随 dial timeout 错误和 Retrying in 10s
rootCause: AGIC pod 无法通过 TCP 443 连接到 Azure Resource Manager (management.azure.com)。通常由 AKS 集群出站流量限制导致：UDR 指向 NVA/Azure Firewall 但未放行 ARM 端点、NSG 阻止了 AzureResourceManager service tag、或 Network Policy 阻止了 AGIC pod 的出站流量
solution: 1. 如 outbound type 为 UDR，检查 NVA/Firewall 规则是否允许到 management.azure.com:443 的流量。2. 如 outbound type 为 LoadBalancer，检查子网路由表是否有 0.0.0.0/0 → NVA 的路由。3. 检查 NSG 出站规则是否阻止了 AzureResourceManager service tag。4. 如启用了 Network Policy，检查是否有策略阻止 AGIC pod 出站到 ARM IP 范围
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod 日志显示 AuthorizationFailed 错误，AGIC 无法获取或更新 Application Gateway 配置
rootCause: AGIC 使用的 Managed Identity 缺少必要的 RBAC 角色分配。需要 Contributor 角色在 Application Gateway 资源上，Reader 角色在 Application Gateway 所在资源组上
solution: 为 AGIC identity 添加角色分配：1. 在 Application Gateway 上分配 Contributor 角色。2. 在 Application Gateway 所在 Resource Group 上分配 Reader 角色。对于 AGIC addon 启用的集群，managed identity 名称格式为 ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AKS virtual node fails with 'error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table' - aci-connector-linux pod fatal error
rootCause: ACI does not support user-defined routing (UDR). The aci-connector has a validation check that blocks subnet delegation to ACI when a route table is associated with the virtual node subnet.
solution: Remove the user-defined routing (route table association) from the subnet used for ACI virtual node. UDR is not supported on the ACI subnet for virtual nodes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError' with 'Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway' followed by 'dial timeout' and 'Retrying in 10s' - AGIC cannot configure Application Gateway
rootCause: Network connectivity from AGIC pod to Azure Resource Manager endpoint (management.azure.com:443) is blocked. Common causes: UDR routing through NVA/Azure Firewall without allowing ARM traffic, NSG denying AzureResourceManager service tag, or Network Policies blocking AGIC pod outbound.
solution: Check and fix egress rules: (1) If UDR outbound, verify NVA/Firewall allows traffic to management.azure.com; (2) If LB outbound with route table 0.0.0.0/0 to NVA, check NVA rules; (3) Verify NSG allows AzureResourceManager service tag; (4) Check Network Policies do not block AGIC pod outbound on port 443.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'AuthorizationFailed' error when trying to fetch or apply configuration to Application Gateway
rootCause: Missing required RBAC role assignments for AGIC identity. AGIC needs: Contributor on Application Gateway scope, Reader on Application Gateway Resource Group.
solution: Create the missing role assignments: assign AGIC managed identity as Contributor on the Application Gateway resource and Reader on the Application Gateway resource group. For AGIC addon clusters, the identity name is 'ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'invalid_client' error - Application Gateway configuration updates fail due to authentication issue
rootCause: The Service Principal secret used by AGIC to authenticate with Azure has expired.
solution: Generate a new secret for the Service Principal and update AGIC to use the new secret following: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'Ignore mutating App Gateway as it is not mutable' and 'Application Gateway is in stopped state' - AGIC cannot apply configuration changes
rootCause: Application Gateway is in Stopped operational state (customer may have stopped it via CLI to reduce costs). AGIC requires AppGW to be in Running state.
solution: Start the Application Gateway: `az network application-gateway start -g <RG> -n <NAME>`. Verify AGIC pod logs show successful deployment after AppGW returns to Running state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AKS virtual node fails: error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table. aci-connector-linux pod logs show level=fatal.
rootCause: ACI does not support user-defined routing (UDR). Subnet for ACI virtual nodes has a route table attached, blocking subnet delegation.
solution: Remove the route table association from the subnet used for ACI virtual node. ACI virtual node subnet must not reference any route table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: AGIC pod logs: ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError / Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway / dial timeout / Retrying in 10s.
rootCause: AGIC pod lacks TCP/443 connectivity to management.azure.com. Blocked by UDR to NVA/Firewall, NSG denying AzureResourceManager service tag, or K8s Network Policies.
solution: Check: 1) NVA/Firewall rules for management.azure.com:443. 2) Route table 0.0.0.0/0 entries. 3) NSG AzureResourceManager service tag. 4) K8s NetworkPolicies for AGIC pod egress.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show AuthorizationFailed error when configuring Application Gateway.
rootCause: AGIC identity missing role assignments. Required: Contributor on AppGW + Reader on AppGW RG. Addon identity: ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>.
solution: Assign Contributor role on AppGW resource and Reader role on AppGW resource group to AGIC identity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show invalid_client error. Application Gateway config updates fail.
rootCause: AGIC uses Service Principal for auth and the SP secret/credential has expired.
solution: Renew SP secret and update AGIC config per MS docs ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs: Ignore mutating App Gateway as it is not mutable / Application Gateway is in stopped state.
rootCause: Application Gateway is in Stopped state. AGIC requires Running state to apply config.
solution: Start AppGW: az network application-gateway start -g <RG> -n <NAME>. Verify: az network application-gateway show --query operationalState.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 404 Not Found when the request path does not match the backend application expected path
rootCause: The URL path in the client request does not match the path on which the backend application is listening. In a reverse proxy setup (AKS/AGIC/AppGW), the request reaches the correct backend but the app cannot find the resource at that path.
solution: Add 'appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-path-prefix' annotation to the ingress resource to rewrite the request path. Alternatively, modify the request URL or reconfigure the app to listen on the expected path.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 404 Not Found

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 502 Bad Gateway during Kubernetes rolling deployments
rootCause: During rolling updates, old pods are terminated before AppGW health probes detect the change, leaving no healthy backend pool members temporarily.
solution: Implement 'Minimizing Downtime During Deployments' recommendations from Azure AGIC docs (https://azure.github.io/application-gateway-kubernetes-ingress/how-tos/minimize-downtime-during-deployments/). Check backend health in Azure Portal (Monitoring > Backend health) or ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 502 Bad Gateway

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout when backend response exceeds configured timeout
rootCause: AppGW enforces a request timeout (default 30s via AGIC annotation). If the backend takes longer to respond, AppGW returns HTTP 504.
solution: Check 'appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/request-timeout' annotation on the ingress (kubectl describe ingress). Increase timeout if backend legitimately needs more time. Verify in Azure Portal under AppGW > Backend Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 504 Gateway Timeout

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'AuthorizationFailed' error when trying to configure Application Gateway
rootCause: AGIC identity missing required role assignments: Contributor on AppGW resource and Reader on AppGW resource group.
solution: Create role assignments: (1) Contributor to AGIC identity on AppGW. (2) Reader to AGIC identity on AppGW RG. For addon clusters, identity name is 'ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'invalid_client' error when authenticating to configure Application Gateway
rootCause: AGIC uses a Service Principal whose secret has expired.
solution: Add a new secret to the Service Principal and update AGIC config. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked/NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics - network profile vk-* still in use with container nics (Virtual Kubelet/ACI)
rootCause: ACI network profile container NICs not cleaned up properly after Virtual Nodes/ACI removal. Known bug (ADO work item 14104, fix PR 4202309) with possible regression.
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via IcM to Support / EEE ACI team with troubleshooting details including AKS operation ID, ARM correlation ID, and NRP HTTP 400 errors against the vk-* network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Unable to delete subnet or parent VNet after AKS cluster deletion - subnet still delegated to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters with ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) remaining
rootCause: When AKS cluster using Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet networking is deleted, the ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) created during cluster provisioning is not automatically removed
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Portal: Virtual Networks > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate subnet to service > None > Save. 2) Or via CLI: az network vnet subnet update --resource-group <RG> --name <subnet> --vnet-name <vnet> --remove delegations. 3) If above fails, use Jarvis actions for undelegation (note: restricted in China/21V, CSS cannot perform Jarvis undelegation in China).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked and inner error NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics - network profile vk-* (Virtual Kubelet/ACI) still in use with container NICs
rootCause: ACI network profile with vk- prefix (Virtual Nodes) still has container NICs bound to it. Known bug with supposed fix (PR 4202309) but may have regression. Tracked in Known Issue 14104.
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via IcM to Support/EEE ACI. Provide: AKS operation ID, correlation ID, ARM logs showing HTTP 400 against Microsoft.Network RP on the vk- network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Cannot delete subnet or VNet after AKS cluster deletion - subnet is delegated to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters with lingering ServiceAssociationLink (SAL). Error: InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted
rootCause: When AKS cluster uses Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet, subnet delegation creates a ServiceAssociationLink (SAL). After cluster deletion, SAL should be auto-removed but sometimes persists.
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Azure Portal: VNet > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate subnet = None > Save. 2) Via CLI: az network vnet subnet update --remove delegations. 3) If above fails, use Jarvis actions for undelegation (CSS can perform, except China which is restricted).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked, inner error NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics. Network profile starting with 'vk-' (Virtual Kubelet/ACI) cannot be deleted because container NICs are still bound.
rootCause: ACI container NICs remain bound to the Virtual Kubelet network profile after cluster/virtual node operations. Possible regression of a 2021 bug in ACI (fix PR 4202309).
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via ICM to 'Support / EEE ACI'. Provide AKS RP operation logs and ARM correlation ID showing HTTP 400 against Microsoft.Network on the 'vk-' network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Cannot delete subnet or parent VNet after removing AKS cluster. Error: InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted, subnet still has ServiceAssociationLink 'AzureKubernetesService' from CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet delegation.
rootCause: When AKS cluster with Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet is deleted, the ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) created by subnet delegation to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters should be auto-removed but sometimes remains.
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Azure Portal: VNet > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate to 'None' > Save. 2) Via CLI: 'az network vnet subnet update --resource-group RG --name subnet --vnet-name vnet --remove delegations'. 3) If above fails: use Jarvis actions for undelegation (CSS except China has access with normal AKSJIT permissions). Ref: CloudNativeCompute wiki Subnet-Delegation-and-Undelegation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady during CRUD/Start operations. kube-proxy pods stuck in Pending/ImagePullBackOff. Kubelet logs: x509 certificate signed by unknown authority when pulling images from mcr.microsoft.com.
rootCause: Third-party firewall performing SSL deep packet inspection intercepts and re-signs TLS certificates for container image pulls, causing x509 certificate validation failure on AKS nodes.
solution: Ask customer to disable SSL packet inspection on firewall for traffic to mcr.microsoft.com and container registries, then retry. Verify via SSH/node-shell into affected node checking kubelet/containerd logs for x509 errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady during CRUD operations. Error mentions pods not in Running status but pod names are user workloads (e.g. 3rd-party metrics-server, custom jobs) deployed in kube-system namespace.
rootCause: AKS RP checks all pods in kube-system namespace to determine control plane readiness. User workload pods in kube-system in Succeeded/Failed/Pending state trigger false-positive ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady errors.
solution: Advise customer to move user workloads out of kube-system namespace per AKS FAQ. Delete or relocate completed Job pods from kube-system. Retry cluster operation after cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch during nodepool upgrade. Autoscaler deletes surge node while AKS RP upgrade is in progress. Existing nodes upgraded in-place instead of creating new surge nodes. Pod eviction takes 5-10 min per node.
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and Cluster Autoscaler. Autoscaler issues forceDelete on surge nodes when RP sets new VMSS capacity. ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade annotation not applied to surge node.
solution: Disable cluster autoscaler before nodepool upgrades (az aks nodepool update --disable-cluster-autoscaler), re-enable after upgrade completes. Known bug: ADO 30155571/31289428.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: Nodepool operations (scale, add disk) fail with MMAExtension error: Enable failed exit code 53, incorrect workspace key. OMSAgent extension failure blocks VMSS operations.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key in AKS omsagent-secret does not match actual LAW primary key. Or NSG rules block outbound to Log Analytics agent endpoints.
solution: Compare workspace key: kubectl get secrets omsagent-secret -n kube-system -o yaml, decode base64, compare with LAW Portal Agents Management Primary Key. If keys match, check NSG/firewall for blocked outbound to LA endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: Newly created nodes not registering with AKS API server. ScaleUpTimedOut after ~15min. VMSS creates VM but node never joins cluster. Persists with CAS disabled and manual scale.
rootCause: vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) accidentally removed from VMSS. Without CSE, nodes lack bootstrap components and cannot register with K8s API server. Required extensions: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension.
solution: Verify all 3 VMSS extensions present. If vmssCSE missing, create new agent pool and migrate workloads (new pool will have correct extensions).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: NAP nodes stuck NotReady in Azure CNI Overlay cluster. NodeNetworkConfig events: primaryIP is nil, Subnet is full. New nodes cannot obtain Pod IPs.
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in CNI Overlay. Each node pre-allocates /24 block (256 IPs). Small Pod CIDR (e.g. /21=8 nodes max) runs out, new nodes cannot get Pod IPs and remain NotReady.
solution: Expand Pod CIDR without cluster recreation: see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand. After expansion, NotReady nodes auto-recover. azure-cns pods redeploy but existing pods unaffected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision subnet is full and pod cidr exhausted

symptom: Cluster or node pool creation fails with SKU Not Found or VM Size Not Available error.
rootCause: Requested VM SKU restricted, unavailable, or not offered in target region/zone due to platform capacity limitations or subscription SKU restrictions.
solution: Check SKU availability: az vm list-skus --location <region> --size <sku>. Check ASC Resource Explorer SKU Restrictions. Use alternative VM size or zone. For quota increase route to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM subscription limit increases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS node scaling operations fail due to vCPU quota exceeded. Total Regional vCPU or VM family quota limit reached.
rootCause: vCPU quotas (Total Regional + VM family) exceeded. VMSS cannot allocate new instances when either limit is reached.
solution: Customer submits quota increase via Azure Portal (standard VM families often auto-approved). For high-demand sizes, route to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM subscription limit increases. Include Sub ID, Region, SKU, Restriction Type, cores requested. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS operations fail with Unable to get log analytics workspace info ResourceNotFound. Error misleadingly points to monitoring addon but may be Container Insights, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender.
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace referenced by AKS addon has been deleted or is inaccessible. AKS pre-flight validates LAW existence, blocking all operations when LAW missing.
solution: Identify which component references missing LAW in ASC. For Defender: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender. For monitoring addon: disable via https://aka.ms/aks-disable-monitoring-addon or re-create LAW and update addon config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operation fails due to missing Log Analytics workspace

symptom: AKS operations fail with ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady/PodInitializing, kube-proxy stuck Pending with ImagePullBackOff, kubelet logs show x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
rootCause: Third-party firewall performing SSL/TLS deep packet inspection between AKS nodes and MCR, causing certificate validation failure when pulling container images.
solution: Ask customer to temporarily disable firewall or third-party SSL inspection device, retry the operation. Verify by SSH into affected node checking kubelet/containerd logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: AKS CRUD/Start operations fail with ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady, error references non-AKS pods (custom metrics-server, user jobs) in kube-system namespace not reaching Running state
rootCause: AKS RP checks ALL pods in kube-system namespace during operations. User workloads in Completed/Failed/Pending state cause AKS RP to misinterpret them as control plane addon failures.
solution: Advise customer to move all user workloads out of kube-system namespace per AKS FAQ. Verify failing pods are customer pods by checking CCP logs and pod labels.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: AKS nodepool upgrade fails with GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch or AgentCountNotMatch. Surge node deleted by cluster autoscaler during upgrade. Pod eviction takes 5-10 min per node.
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and cluster autoscaler: autoscaler forceDeletes surge nodes while RP is upgrading. ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade annotation not applied to surge node. Bug 30155571, Issue 31289428.
solution: Disable cluster autoscaler before nodepool upgrades, re-enable after completion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: AKS nodepool operations fail with ProvisioningError on MMAExtension exit code 53: Installation failed due to incorrect workspace key
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key in AKS (omsagent-secret) mismatches actual key, OR node cannot reach Log Analytics endpoints due to NSG/firewall.
solution: 1) Compare omsagent-secret (base64 decoded) with Log Analytics Primary Key. 2) Check outbound NSG rules for LA endpoints. 3) Engage Azure Monitor team if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: New AKS nodes not registering with API server. VMSS creates VMs but they never join cluster. Autoscaler ScaleUpTimedOut after 15 min.
rootCause: vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) accidentally removed from VMSS. Without CSE, nodes cannot bootstrap kubelet/CNI. Required: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension.
solution: Create new agent pool, migrate workloads. Verify new VMSS has all 3 required extensions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: AKS NAP/Karpenter nodes stuck NotReady. NodeNetworkConfig events: primaryIP is nil, Subnet is full. NodeClaim Ready=Unknown.
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in CNI Overlay. Each node pre-allocates /24 (256 IPs). Small Pod CIDR (e.g. /21=8 nodes max) causes IP allocation failure.
solution: Expand Pod CIDR via Azure CNI Overlay expansion (learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand). NotReady nodes auto-recover.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision subnet is full and pod cidr exhausted

symptom: AKS cluster/nodepool creation or scaling fails with SkuNotAvailable or VM size not available error
rootCause: Requested VM SKU restricted in subscription, not offered in target region/zone, or has zonal capacity limitations.
solution: 1) az vm list-skus to check availability. 2) ASC Resource Explorer > SKU Restrictions. 3) Request quota increase or select alternative VM. 4) Transfer to SAP Compute-VM subscription limit increases if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS node scaling fails with QuotaExceeded. Exceeds vCPU quota for VM size family or total regional quota.
rootCause: Subscription vCPU quota exhausted. Azure two-tier quota: VM-family-specific + total regional vCPU limit.
solution: 1) Submit quota increase via Portal (auto-approved for standard families). 2) Transfer to SAP Compute-VM subscription limit increases. Include: Sub ID, Region, SKU, Restriction Type, cores. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS operations fail with ResourceNotFound: Unable to get log analytics workspace info. Error misleadingly references monitoring addon but may be Azure Defender or Container Insights.
rootCause: LAW referenced by monitoring addon, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender has been deleted or is inaccessible.
solution: 1) In ASC, identify which component uses the LAW. 2) Defender-linked: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender. 3) OMSAgent-linked: disable monitoring addon, re-enable with valid LAW. 4) Custom LAW: use --defender-config with logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operation fails due to missing Log Analytics workspace
21vNote: Defender for Containers not supported in 21Vianet. OMSAgent scenario may still apply.

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady error during CRUD/Start operations: kube-proxy pods stuck in Pending/PodInitializing with ImagePullBackOff, kubelet logs show 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' when pulling mcr.microsoft.com images
rootCause: Third-party firewall or network appliance performing SSL/TLS deep packet inspection (DPI) between AKS nodes and MCR, inserting its own certificate. Containerd/kubelet rejects the substituted certificate as untrusted.
solution: 1) SSH/node-shell into the affected node, check kubelet logs for x509 certificate errors; 2) Ask customer to temporarily disable SSL packet inspection on their firewall/proxy for MCR endpoints; 3) Retry the cluster operation after disabling DPI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady error during CRUD/Start operations: non-AKS user pods (e.g. create-cmr-metrics-server) deployed in kube-system namespace are not in Running state, blocking cluster operations
rootCause: AKS RP validates all pods in kube-system namespace during operations. User workloads deployed in kube-system (especially those with names matching system components like metrics-server) that are in Completed/Failed/Pending state cause validation to fail.
solution: 1) Identify the failing pods in ContextActivity logs (look for non-AKS pod names); 2) Ask customer to move user workloads out of kube-system namespace; 3) Per AKS FAQ, do NOT deploy user workloads in kube-system namespace; 4) Temporarily delete/move problematic pods to unblock operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady kube system namespace for user pods

symptom: During nodepool upgrade, autoscaler deletes the surge node causing GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch / AgentCountNotMatch error; existing nodes upgraded in-place instead of creating new surge nodes; pod eviction takes 5-10 minutes per node
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and cluster autoscaler. AKS RP failed to annotate the surge node with ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade tag, allowing autoscaler to issue forceDelete on the surge node while upgrade was in progress.
solution: 1) Recommend customer disable cluster autoscaler before performing nodepool upgrades; 2) Re-enable autoscaler after upgrade completes; 3) Bug tracked: ADO work items 30155571 / 31289428; 4) Check AsyncContextActivity for surge node creation and CRP logs for VMSS scale events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: AKS nodepool operations (scale, update, add disk) fail with MMAExtension error: Enable failed with exit code 53 - Installation failed due to incorrect workspace key
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key configured in AKS (stored in omsagent-secret in kube-system) does not match the actual workspace primary key in Azure portal, or the node lacks outbound connectivity to required Log Analytics endpoints.
solution: 1) Get LAW resource ID from az aks show addonProfiles.omsagent; 2) Compare workspace key from kubectl get secrets omsagent-secret -n kube-system -o yaml (base64 decode) with LAW portal Agents Management > Primary Key; 3) If keys mismatch, update the secret; 4) Verify outbound NSG rules allow access to LAW endpoints; 5) Engage Azure Monitor team if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: Newly created nodes fail to register with AKS API server; autoscaler scales up VMSS VMs but they never join the cluster; ScaleUpTimedOut warning after 15 minutes; issue persists with manual scale-up
rootCause: VMSS Custom Script Extension (vmssCSE) was accidentally removed from the VMSS. Without CSE, nodes cannot bootstrap essential components and register with the Kubernetes API server.
solution: 1) Verify all 3 required extensions are present on AKS VMSS: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension; 2) If vmssCSE is missing, create a new agent pool and migrate workloads; 3) Do not manually remove VMSS extensions from AKS-managed scale sets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: NAP (Node Auto Provision / Karpenter) scaled nodes stuck in NotReady state; NodeNetworkConfig events show 'primaryIP is nil' and 'Subnet is full' errors; NodeClaim status Unknown; azure-cns cannot create network container
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in Azure CNI Overlay cluster. Each node pre-allocates a /24 block (256 IPs) from Pod CIDR. When Pod CIDR is too small (e.g. /21 supports only 8 nodes), additional nodes cannot obtain IP allocation.
solution: 1) Check NodeNetworkConfig events for 'Subnet is full' and 'primaryIP is nil' errors; 2) Expand Pod CIDR using az aks update per docs (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand); 3) After expansion, NotReady nodes auto-recover; azure-cns pods redeploy but existing pods unaffected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision subnet is full and pod cidr exhausted

symptom: AKS cluster or nodepool creation fails with SkuNotAvailable / VM Size Not Available error; requested VM SKU not offered in the target region or zone
rootCause: Requested VM size is not available in the subscription due to regional restrictions, zonal limitations, or platform capacity constraints.
solution: 1) Check SKU availability: az vm list-skus --location <region> --size <size> --all; 2) Check ASC Resource Explorer > Subscription > RP Details > SKU Restrictions; 3) Advise alternative VM size or zone; 4) Transfer to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM (cores-vCPUs) subscription limit increases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS node scaling, creation, or upgrade fails due to vCPU quota exceeded; operation triggers new node creation but exceeds Total Regional vCPUs or VM family quota
rootCause: vCPU quotas are arranged in two tiers per subscription per region: Total Regional vCPUs and VM size family cores. When node creation would exceed either quota, VMSS deployment is blocked.
solution: 1) Customer submits direct quota request (many standard VM family increases auto-approved); 2) Check Usage + quotas in Azure portal; 3) For large/high-demand VMs, manual review required; 4) Transfer to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM (cores-vCPUs); 5) Include: Subscription ID, Region, SKU Size, Restriction Type, cores requested. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS operations (scale/update/upgrade) fail with 'Unable to get log analytics workspace info. ResourceNotFound' error pointing to monitoring addon; error is misleading as actual component may be Azure Defender or Container Insights
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace (LAW) referenced by AKS addon (Container Insights, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender) has been deleted or is inaccessible. Error message misleadingly blames the monitoring addon even when Defender is the actual consumer.
solution: 1) In ASC main cluster view, search LAW resource ID to identify which component uses it; 2) Option A: Disable/re-enable Defender: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender; 3) Option B: Update in-place with existing LAW via config.json; 4) If LAW is for OMSAgent, see separate TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operation fails due to missing Log Analytics workspace

symptom: AKS cluster CRUD operations (upgrade, scale) fail with InternalServerError/GetSKUStoreError. Underlying error: InvalidAuthenticationToken - The received access token is not valid: at least one of the claims puid or altsecid or oid should be present
rootCause: The Service Principal linked to the AKS PG well-known App ID (7319c514-..., Azure Container Service - AKS) was deleted or soft-deleted from the customer tenant, breaking AKS RP operations when calling CRP to get VM SKUs.
solution: Re-register the AKS resource provider to recreate the SP: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService. If SP is in Soft-Delete mode, customer can try recovering it (may require collaboration with Azure Identity team). Confirm via AKS ContextActivity Kusto table and ARM Traces, check SP status in Azure TenantExplorer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations Fail with code GetSKUStoreError InvalidAuthenticationToken

symptom: AKS cluster operations (update, scale up) fail with error code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts - Load balancer profile allocated ports X is not in an allowable range given the number of nodes and IPs provisioned
rootCause: The specified node count, number of outbound frontend IP addresses and number of SNAT ports per node do not add up to a viable configuration. Each outbound IP provides 64000 SNAT ports, and the total available ports must cover all nodes including surge nodes during upgrades.
solution: Use the formula: 64000 ports per IP / outbound_ports_per_node * number_of_outbound_IPs = max_nodes. Increase outbound frontend IPs (each adds 64000 ports) or reduce allocated outbound ports per node. Account for maxSurge buffer nodes during upgrades (default 1).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations fail with code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts

symptom: AKS cluster create/deploy/scale fails with QuotaExceeded - Operation results in exceeding quota limits of Core. Maximum allowed: N, Current in use: M, Additional requested: X
rootCause: Customer subscription has exceeded its CPU core quota for the region. The required VMs for the operation exceed the allowed core count.
solution: Customer should request a CPU core quota increase via Azure Portal. After quota increase, retry the operation. For upgrades, if portal shows upgraded version but operation failed mid-way, re-run upgrade via CLI. Confirm via ASC subscription quotas view or AKSprod Kusto tables.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Quota limits are exceeded

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with QuotaExceeded/ManagedClusterCountExceedsQuotaLimit - Operation results in exceeding quota limits for managed clusters. Maximum allowed: N, Current usage: M
rootCause: AKS instated per-subscription per-region quota limits on managed cluster count (effective September 2, 2025). Customer has reached the maximum number of clusters allowed.
solution: Customer should request quota increase via Azure Portal Quotas page. Must file under support path: Azure/Subscription limits and quota/Azure Kubernetes Service (not AKS support path). Quota ICMs auto-created against AzureKubernetesService/ClusterQuotaRequests team (all Sev 3). Decline for student/free-trial/DreamSpark subscriptions; route internal requests to AZURECONTAINERSERVICE/Platform.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/QuotaExceeded QuotaExceededManagedCluster

symptom: AKS nodepool scale or upgrade fails with image not found - InvalidParameter: images referenced from the deployment template are not found (Publisher: MicrosoftWindowsServer, Offer: WindowsServer, Sku: ..., Version: ...)
rootCause: The Kubernetes version is outdated/no longer supported and the base VM images for the node pool are no longer available in the image gallery, preventing new node creation.
solution: 1) Create new nodepool with supported K8s version. 2) Migrate pods to new nodes (apply custom labels if needed). 3) Drain and cordon old nodes: kubectl drain <node>. 4) Delete old nodepool. 5) If cluster is in Failed provisioning state, upgrade cluster to current K8s version. 6) Verify cluster provisioning status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Scale or upgrade failed with image not found

symptom: AKS node pool operations (scale, upgrade, reconcile) fail with VMExtensionHandlerNonTransientError for unsupported VM extensions (e.g., DependencyAgentLinux, AzureMonitorLinuxAgent) manually installed on VMSS
rootCause: Custom VM extensions were manually installed on the AKS node pool VMSS outside of AKS control. AKS cannot validate or maintain these extensions, causing non-retriable reconcile failures that block all CRUD operations on the affected node pool.
solution: 1) Identify unsupported VM extensions on the VMSS (check AppLens detector or Azure Portal VMSS Extensions). 2) Remove extensions directly from VMSS (Portal/CLI/ARM). 3) Perform node pool reconcile or retry failed operation. 4) If removal not possible, recreate node pool without the extension. Prevention: never manually install VM extensions on AKS VMSS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/UnsupportedVMExtensions

symptom: AKS cluster stays in Starting state, nodes not visible after start or scale-up despite VMSS instances being created. kubectl get nodes shows missing nodes.
rootCause: The vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) was removed/deleted from the VMSS of the node pool. Without this extension, new nodes cannot complete provisioning and join the cluster.
solution: 1) Abort any ongoing AKS operation: az aks operation-abort -n <name> -g <rg>. 2) Confirm abort succeeded (Status=Canceled): az aks operation show-latest. 3) Upgrade node pool image (works even if already on latest): az aks nodepool upgrade --node-image-only --cluster-name <name> -g <rg> --name <pool>. This reinstalls the vmssCSE extension. Verify with Kusto query checking for 'has no vmss cse extension' in AKSprod.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/vmssCSE deleted

symptom: AKS cluster CRUD operations (upgrade/scale) fail with InternalServerError, GetSKUStoreError, or InvalidAuthenticationToken: The received access token is not valid - at least one of the claims puid or altsecid or oid should be present
rootCause: The Service Principal linked to the AKS PG well-known App ID (7319c514-...) was deleted or soft-deleted from the customer tenant, breaking AKS RP ability to call ARM/CRP
solution: Re-register the AKS resource provider to recreate the SP: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService. If SP is still in soft-delete, recover it (may require collab with Azure Identity team)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations Fail with code GetSKUStoreError InvalidAuthenticationToken

symptom: AKS operations fail with InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts: allocated ports X is not in an allowable range given the number of nodes and IPs provisioned
rootCause: Node count x allocated outbound ports per node exceeds total available SNAT ports from outbound public IPs (each IP provides 64000 ports). Default: 1024 ports/node for <=50 nodes
solution: Validate config with formula: 64000 x outbound_IPs / ports_per_node >= total_node_count (including maxSurge during upgrades). Either increase outbound IP count or reduce allocated ports per node
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations fail with code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts

symptom: AKS scale/upgrade/create fails with QuotaExceeded: Operation results in exceeding quota limits of Core. Maximum allowed: N, Current in use: M, Additional requested: X
rootCause: Subscription compute (CPU vCPU/core) quota exceeded for the target VM size or region
solution: Customer requests core quota increase via Azure Portal (per-vm-quota-requests). After quota increase, retry the operation. If upgrade was in progress, re-run upgrade via CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Quota limits are exceeded

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with QuotaExceeded ManagedClusterCountExceedsQuotaLimit: exceeding quota limits for managed clusters per subscription per region
rootCause: AKS managed cluster count per subscription per region quota exceeded (enforced since 2025-09-02). Distinct from compute/core quota - limits number of AKS clusters
solution: File support ticket to Azure/Subscription limits and quota/Azure Kubernetes Service (NOT AKS support path). ICM auto-created to AzureKubernetesService/ClusterQuotaRequests. Quota increase via Geneva Action PutSubscriptionLimit. Decline for student/free-trial subs; internal subs route to Platform team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/QuotaExceeded QuotaExceededManagedCluster

symptom: AKS scaling or upgrade fails with image not found: InvalidParameter - images referenced from deployment template are not found (Publisher/Offer/Sku/Version no longer available)
rootCause: Kubernetes version is outdated/unsupported and base VM images (e.g. WindowsServer 2019-datacenter-core-smalldisk) are no longer available in marketplace
solution: 1) Create new nodepool with supported K8s version 2) Migrate pods (check custom labels with kubectl get nodes --show-labels) 3) kubectl drain old nodes 4) Delete old nodepool 5) Upgrade cluster to same or higher version 6) Verify provisioning state
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Scale or upgrade failed with image not found

symptom: AKS node pool operations (scale/upgrade/reconcile) fail with VMExtensionHandlerNonTransientError for unsupported VM extension (e.g. DependencyAgentLinux, AzureMonitorLinuxAgent installed outside AKS)
rootCause: Unsupported VM extensions manually installed on AKS VMSS outside AKS control. AKS cannot validate/maintain extension state, causing non-retriable reconcile failures
solution: 1) Identify unsupported extensions on VMSS (AppLens detector available) 2) Remove extensions from VMSS via Portal/CLI/ARM 3) Reconcile or retry failed operation 4) If removal impossible, recreate nodepool. Prevention: never manually install VM extensions on AKS VMSS
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/UnsupportedVMExtensions

symptom: AKS cluster stuck in Starting state or autoscaler nodes not visible despite VMSS instances being created. Kusto shows: vmss <name> instance N has no vmss cse extension
rootCause: The vmssCSE (CustomScript) extension was removed/deleted from the VMSS backing the AKS node pool. Without this extension, nodes cannot complete provisioning and join the cluster
solution: 1) Abort ongoing operation: az aks operation-abort -n <cluster> -g <rg> 2) Confirm abort successful (Status=Canceled) 3) Upgrade nodepool image: az aks nodepool upgrade --node-image-only --cluster-name <cluster> -g <rg> --name <pool> to reinstall vmssCSE
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/vmssCSE deleted

symptom: AKS CRUD operations (upgrade/scale) fail with InternalServerError. Underlying error: InvalidAuthenticationToken - access token missing claims puid/altsecid/oid, or GetSKUStoreError when AKS calls CRP for VM SKUs
rootCause: Service Principal linked to the AKS PG App ID was removed from the customer tenant, breaking AKS RP ability to authenticate against ARM/CRP on behalf of the cluster.
solution: 1) Confirm via AKS ContextActivity Kusto query filtering by operationID for InvalidAuthenticationToken messages. 2) Cross-reference ARM Traces table using correlationID to identify affected TenantID and AppID. 3) Re-create the Service Principal in the customer tenant for the AKS PG App ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations Fail with code GetSKUStoreError InvalidAuthenticationToken

symptom: AKS operations fail with InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts - allocated outbound ports not in allowable range given node count and outbound IPs. SNAT port exhaustion possible.
rootCause: Load balancer outbound port allocation formula violated: allocatedPortsPerNode * totalNodeCount exceeds 64000 * numberOfOutboundIPs. Each outbound IP provides 64000 SNAT ports; default 1024 ports per node for <=50 nodes.
solution: Before any scale/update operation, verify formula: 64000 * outbound_IPs / ports_per_node >= total_nodes (including maxSurge for upgrades). Fix by either: increasing outbound IP count, or reducing allocated outbound ports per node.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operations fail with code InvalidLoadBalancerProfileAllocatedOutboundPorts

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with QuotaExceeded/ManagedClusterCountExceedsQuotaLimit - exceeds per-subscription per-region managed cluster count quota (enforced since Sep 2, 2025)
rootCause: AKS cluster count quota limit reached. AKS enforces maximum cluster count per subscription per region. Ineligible subscription types (Free Trial, Students, DreamSpark) cannot request increases.
solution: Customer must file NEW support ticket via correct path: Azure/Subscription limits and quota/Azure Kubernetes Service. Do NOT accept via AKS support path. ICM auto-created to AzureKubernetesService/ClusterQuotaRequests (Sev 3). Decline ineligible sub types, route internal to AZURECONTAINERSERVICE/Platform.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/QuotaExceeded QuotaExceededManagedCluster

symptom: AKS nodepool scale/upgrade fails with InvalidParameter: images referenced from deployment template are not found (e.g. WindowsServer 2019-datacenter-core-smalldisk)
rootCause: Kubernetes version is outdated/unsupported and the base VM images are no longer available in Azure marketplace. Old nodepool cannot create new nodes because the image reference is stale.
solution: 1) Create new nodepool with supported K8s version. 2) Copy custom labels. 3) Drain+cordon old nodes. 4) Delete old nodepool. 5) If cluster still Failed, az aks upgrade to same/higher version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Scale or upgrade failed with image not found

symptom: AKS nodepool operations (scale/upgrade/reconcile) fail with VMExtensionHandlerNonTransientError - non-retriable failure from unsupported VM extension (e.g. DependencyAgentLinux) on VMSS
rootCause: Custom VM extensions manually installed at VMSS level outside AKS control. AKS manages VMSS lifecycle and cannot validate/maintain custom extensions, causing non-retriable reconcile failures.
solution: 1) Identify via AppLens detector or VMSS Extensions tab. 2) Remove extensions from VMSS (Portal/CLI/ARM). 3) Retry or reconcile. 4) If impossible, recreate nodepool. Never manually install VM extensions on AKS VMSS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/UnsupportedVMExtensions

symptom: AKS cluster stuck in Starting state or autoscaler scale-up creates VMSS instances but new nodes never appear. Kusto shows 'has no vmss cse extension' errors.
rootCause: vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) was removed from VMSS of the affected nodepool(s). Without vmssCSE, nodes cannot bootstrap and register with API server.
solution: 1) Abort ongoing operation: az aks operation-abort. 2) Confirm abort (Status=Canceled). 3) Upgrade nodepool image: az aks nodepool upgrade --node-image-only. This restores vmssCSE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/vmssCSE deleted

symptom: AKS upgrade/update fails with FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup. agic-overlay-extension-config OEC resource stuck in Terminating state in kube-system namespace with DNC finalizer
rootCause: On Azure CNI Overlay clusters with AGIC/AGC, the OverlayExtensionConfig resource enters deletion but DNC-managed finalizer (finalizers.acn.azure.com/dnc-operations) cannot be removed due to DNC-RC cleanup failure (e.g. missing Cosmos DB SubnetTokenMap entry). OEC stays stuck, blocking control plane operation
solution: 1) Verify OEC stuck: kubectl get oec -n kube-system agic-overlay-extension-config -o yaml (check deletionTimestamp + finalizer present); 2) Confirm no pending DNC cleanup activity via Kusto; 3) Only if AGIC extension is NOT actively in use: remove finalizer with kubectl edit/patch to unblock deletion; 4) If AGIC is actively in use, do NOT remove finalizer - escalate to PG
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/AKS Upgrade FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with errors referencing Log Analytics workspace: 'Unable to get log analytics workspace info' (ResourceGroupNotFound/404), AddContainerInsightsSolutionError, or MMAExtension provisioning failure with exit code 53
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace or its resource group was deleted, or workspace key is invalid. The monitoring addon cannot reconcile, causing cluster provisioning failure
solution: Disable monitoring addon via CLI: az aks disable-addons -a monitoring. Then re-enable through Azure Portal with a valid LA workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with 'Reconcile standard load balancer failed' - InvalidResourceReference: frontendIPConfigurations resource was not found
rootCause: Load balancer FrontEndIpConfiguration was accidentally removed by customer, causing LB reconciliation failure
solution: Manually add back the FrontEndIpConfiguration with the same configuration ID referenced in the error message
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with SubnetIsFull - subnet does not have enough capacity for N IP addresses
rootCause: The subnet available IP address space is exhausted. Cannot provision new nodes/pods
solution: Either: 1) Reduce node count to free up address space; 2) Create temporary nodepool in a different subnet, migrate workloads, then recreate original nodepool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS operations fail with DeploymentQuotaExceeded - creating deployment would exceed quota of 800. Current deployment count is 800
rootCause: ARM deployment history limit (800 per resource group) reached in the MC_ resource group
solution: Delete old ARM deployments from the MC_ resource group to free quota. Can self-service via Azure Portal Quota blade or delete deployments via CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with RequestDisallowedByPolicy - resource was disallowed by Azure Policy (e.g. missing required tags)
rootCause: Azure Policy assignments on the subscription/resource group block AKS resource creation (disks, IPs, etc.) due to unmet policy conditions
solution: Guide customer to locate the blocking policy assignment (shown in error details), then either: 1) Create policy exemption for the MC_ resource group; 2) Adjust policy rule to allow AKS-managed resources
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with ScopeLocked during upgrade/scale - cannot perform delete/write operation on locked resources in MC_ resource group
rootCause: Resource locks (CanNotDelete/ReadOnly) applied to nodes, disks, or other resources in the MC_ managed resource group prevent AKS from managing its infrastructure
solution: Remove or temporarily disable resource locks on the MC_ resource group and its resources, then retry the AKS operation. Locks on MC_ group are not recommended
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster update/upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed - 'virtual network cannot be linked to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces' for privatelink.<region>.azmk8s.io
rootCause: Customer created a private DNS zone with the exact same name as AKS private DNS zone (*.privatelink.<region>.azmk8s.io) in a different subscription and linked it to the same VNet. AKS RP cannot find the correct zone
solution: Remove the VNet link between the AKS cluster VNet and the duplicate private DNS zone (the one in the wrong subscription). Then reconcile the cluster and retry the operation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Create or update virtual network link failure

symptom: AKS upgrade fails with CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed - 'Cannot complete the operation since the provided disk size N is lesser than the existing disk size M' (OperationNotAllowed)
rootCause: Customer manually resized VMSS OS disks at the IaaS/VMSS level, creating discrepancy between AKS control plane expected disk size and actual VMSS disk size. Manual VMSS changes are unsupported
solution: 1) Identify affected node pools by running per-nodepool upgrade; 2) Compare AKS config vs CRP config via Jarvis/SAW; 3) Delete and recreate affected node pools with correct disk size via az CLI; 4) Re-run upgrade
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Disk size mismatch causes upgrade failures

symptom: AKS cluster update or delete fails with EtagMismatch - 'Operation is not allowed: Another operation is in progress' (PutManagedClusterAndComponents_FailedPrecondition)
rootCause: Another operation is already in progress or the cluster resource entered an inconsistent state due to previous failed operations, causing etag mismatch
solution: 1) Reconcile the cluster: az resource update --ids <AKS cluster resource ID>; 2) If fails, attempt reconcile via Geneva action (SAW required); 3) If persists, file IcM to AKS PG
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/EtagMismatch error when updating or deleting AKS cluster

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods schedule on unintended node pools. Custom taints previously applied at the node level are missing. No errors, upgrade succeeds but scheduling behavior changes
rootCause: AKS upgrades recreate nodes. Taints applied manually at the node level (kubectl taint) are not persisted and are lost when nodes are replaced. Only nodepool-level taints survive upgrades
solution: 1) Set taints at node pool level: az aks nodepool update --node-taints workload=restricted:NoSchedule; 2) Verify pod tolerations match; 3) Add nodeSelector/nodeAffinity for deterministic placement; 4) Always configure taints at nodepool level before future upgrades
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Lost Taint After Upgrade

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods unexpectedly schedule onto system or untainted node pools despite having tolerations configured. No errors but workloads land on wrong nodes
rootCause: Tolerations only ALLOW pods to schedule on tainted nodes but do NOT force or prefer placement. Without nodeSelector or nodeAffinity, the scheduler may place pods on any eligible untainted node
solution: Use BOTH tolerations AND nodeSelector/nodeAffinity: 1) Define matching toleration for target nodepool taint; 2) Add nodeSelector (agentpool: workloadpool) or nodeAffinity to enforce placement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Misunderstanding tolerations behavior

symptom: AKS upgrade or update fails with FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup. The agic-overlay-extension-config OEC resource is stuck in deletion in kube-system namespace with dnc-operations finalizer.
rootCause: On Azure CNI Overlay clusters with AGIC/AGC, DNC-RC cleanup failed (e.g. missing Cosmos DB SubnetTokenMap entry), leaving the finalizers.acn.azure.com/dnc-operations finalizer on the OEC resource and blocking control plane operations.
solution: 1) Verify cluster uses overlay networking and agic-overlay-extension-config is stuck in deletion with dnc-operations finalizer. 2) Confirm no pending DNC cleanup activity via Kusto. 3) kubectl edit oec agic-overlay-extension-config -n kube-system and remove the finalizers block. 4) OEC auto-deletes, then reconcile the cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/AKS Upgrade FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state with errors referencing Log Analytics workspace: ResourceGroupNotFound, AddContainerInsightsSolutionError, or MMAExtension failure with exit code 53.
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace or its resource group referenced by the monitoring addon was deleted, moved, or misconfigured.
solution: Disable the monitoring addon via Azure CLI (az aks disable-addons -a monitoring), then re-enable it through Azure Portal pointing to a valid workspace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with SubnetIsFull error: subnet does not have enough capacity for required IP addresses.
rootCause: The subnet assigned to the AKS cluster has insufficient available IP addresses for the requested node scaling or upgrade operation.
solution: Either reduce node count to free address space, or create a temporary nodepool in a different subnet, migrate workloads, then recreate the original nodepool with proper subnet sizing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with ScopeLocked error: cannot perform delete/write operations because of resource locks on the MC_ resource group.
rootCause: Resource locks (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) applied to the AKS managed resource group (MC_*) or associated resources prevent AKS from modifying underlying IaaS resources.
solution: Remove the corresponding resource locks from the MC_ resource group and associated resources, then retry the AKS operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with RequestDisallowedByPolicy: Azure Policy blocks resource creation or modification required by AKS managed resources.
rootCause: Azure Policy assignments enforce tag, naming, or configuration requirements that conflict with AKS-managed resources in the MC_ resource group.
solution: Locate the blocking policy assignments via error details, then create a policy exemption for the MC_ resource group or adjust policy rules to exclude AKS-managed resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster update/upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed: 'A virtual network cannot be linked to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces' for privatelink.<region>.azmk8s.io zones.
rootCause: Customer created a private DNS zone with the same name as the AKS private DNS zone in a different subscription and linked it to the same VNet, causing ambiguous DNS resolution.
solution: Remove the VNet link between the AKS VNet and the duplicate private DNS zone in the wrong subscription, then reconcile the cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Create or update virtual network link failure

symptom: AKS upgrade fails with CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed: 'Cannot complete the operation since the provided disk size N is lesser than the existing disk size M' due to OS disk size discrepancy.
rootCause: Customer manually resized VMSS OS disks at the IaaS level outside the AKS API, causing mismatch between the AKS control plane expected disk size and the actual VMSS configuration.
solution: Identify affected node pools by running per-nodepool upgrades. Delete and recreate them (same name) with the correct OS disk size via az CLI, then re-run the AKS upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Disk size mismatch causes upgrade failures

symptom: Unable to update or delete AKS cluster due to EtagMismatch error: 'Operation is not allowed: Another operation is in progress' with subcode PutManagedClusterAndComponents_FailedPrecondition.
rootCause: A concurrent or previous AKS operation left the cluster in an inconsistent state, causing etag mismatch between the client request and the current resource version.
solution: Reconcile the cluster: 1) Customer runs 'az resource update --ids <AKS resource id>'. 2) CSS can reconcile from Geneva. 3) If persists, file IcM to AKS PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/EtagMismatch error when updating or deleting AKS cluster

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods schedule on unintended node pools. Manually applied node taints are lost, causing workloads to land on system or shared pools with no explicit errors.
rootCause: AKS upgrades recreate nodes. Taints applied manually at the node level (kubectl taint) are not persisted through node recreation; only node pool-level taints survive.
solution: Configure taints at the node pool level: 'az aks nodepool update --node-taints key=value:NoSchedule'. Combine with nodeSelector/nodeAffinity and tolerations for deterministic scheduling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Lost Taint After Upgrade

symptom: After AKS upgrade, pods schedule onto system or untainted node pools unexpectedly despite having tolerations configured. No errors, but scheduling behavior changes.
rootCause: Pods configured with tolerations only without nodeSelector or nodeAffinity. Tolerations only ALLOW scheduling on tainted nodes but do NOT force or prefer it; the scheduler may place pods on any eligible node.
solution: Use both tolerations AND nodeSelector/nodeAffinity. Add nodeSelector: {agentpool: <target-pool>} to pod spec alongside toleration to ensure deterministic scheduling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Misunderstanding tolerations behavior

symptom: AKS upgrade/update fails with FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup; agic-overlay-extension-config OEC stuck in deletion with dnc-operations finalizer
rootCause: On Azure CNI Overlay clusters with AGIC/AGC, the OEC cleanup fails because the DNC-RC reconciler cannot remove the finalizer (missing Cosmos DB SubnetTokenMap entry), leaving OEC in terminating state
solution: 1) Verify cluster uses overlay and OEC is stuck in deletion with finalizers.acn.azure.com/dnc-operations. 2) Confirm no pending DNC cleanup via Kusto. 3) kubectl edit oec and remove finalizers block. 4) Retry via cluster reconcile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/AKS Upgrade FailedToWaitForOverlayExtensionConfigCleanup

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with SubnetIsFull error: Subnet does not have enough capacity for N IP addresses
rootCause: The subnet assigned to the AKS node pool has exhausted its available IP address space
solution: Either reduce the node count to free up address space, or create a temporary nodepool in a temporary subnet, move workloads over, and recreate the initial nodepool
