sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with RequestDisallowedByPolicy error: resource was disallowed by Azure Policy
rootCause: Customer has Azure Policy assignments (e.g. requiring specific tags) that block AKS resource operations
solution: Guide the customer to locate the blocking policy assignments, then either create a policy exemption or adjust the policy rules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster operations fail with ScopeLocked error: resource group cannot perform operation because scope is locked
rootCause: Customer has resource locks (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) on the AKS resource group preventing AKS operations like node deletion during upgrade
solution: Remove the corresponding resource locks on the AKS managed resource group (MC_*) and/or the AKS resource itself
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster enters Failed state due to Log Analytics workspace errors: Unable to get log analytics workspace info or AddContainerInsightsSolutionError
rootCause: The Log Analytics workspace referenced by the AKS monitoring addon was deleted, moved, or has incorrect workspace key/configuration
solution: Disable the monitoring addon via Azure CLI (az aks disable-addons --addons monitoring), then re-enable it through Azure Portal with a valid workspace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade causes workload disruption or stalls when system node pool has only one node
rootCause: System node pools cannot configure maxUnavailable; during upgrades at least one additional system node is needed to maintain availability of kube-system pods
solution: Ensure system node pools contain at least 2 nodes (3 recommended for production) before performing upgrades
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Compilation of Cluster In Failed State

symptom: AKS cluster update/upgrade fails with CreateOrUpdateVirtualNetworkLinkFailed: A virtual network cannot be linked to multiple zones with overlapping namespaces
rootCause: Customer created a private DNS zone with the same name as the AKS-managed private DNS zone in a different subscription and linked it to the AKS cluster VNet
solution: Remove the link between the AKS cluster VNet and the duplicate private DNS zone in the wrong subscription, then reconcile the cluster
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Create or update virtual network link failure

symptom: AKS upgrade fails with CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed: Cannot complete the operation since the provided disk size 128 is lesser than the existing disk size 256
rootCause: Customer manually resized VMSS OS disks at the IaaS level, causing a mismatch between AKS control plane expected config and actual VMSS disk size
solution: Identify affected node pools by per-nodepool upgrade. Compare AKS config vs CRP config via Jarvis. Delete and recreate affected node pools with correct OS disk size, then re-run AKS upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Disk size mismatch causes upgrade failures

symptom: Unable to update or delete AKS cluster due to EtagMismatch error: Operation is not allowed, Another operation is in progress
rootCause: A concurrent or previous operation left the cluster resource in an inconsistent state, causing etag precondition failures
solution: 1) Reconcile: az resource update --ids <AKS cluster id>. 2) Or use Geneva action. 3) If persists, file IcM to AKS PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/EtagMismatch error when updating or deleting AKS cluster

symptom: After AKS upgrade, node taints are lost and pods schedule on unintended node pools without errors
rootCause: Taints were applied manually at the node level (kubectl taint) instead of at the node pool level. AKS upgrades recreate nodes, so manually applied taints are not persisted.
solution: Configure taints at the node pool level using az aks nodepool update --node-taints. Combine with nodeSelector or nodeAffinity for deterministic scheduling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Lost Taint After Upgrade

symptom: AKS PUT operations (upgrade/update) fail with OverlaymgrReconcileError / OverlayMgrAPIRequestFailed caused by Kyverno webhook failures. Error: failed calling webhook validate.kyverno.svc-fail: no endpoints available for service kyverno-svc
rootCause: Kyverno validatingwebhookconfiguration is configured with failurePolicy=Fail, but the Kyverno service pods are down or have no endpoints. This blocks API server requests including overlay manager reconciliation, causing OverlaymgrReconcileError with context deadline exceeded.
solution: 1. Remove readiness/liveness probes from kube-apiserver container. 2. Access overlay cluster and delete the kyverno-resource-validating-webhook-cfg ValidatingWebhookConfiguration. 3. Verify APIService v1beta3.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io is created. 4. Use Geneva action to reconcile the cluster. 5. Wait for new API Servers to come up healthy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/OverlaymgrReconcileError caused by kyverno webhook failures

symptom: AKS PUT operations fail with OverlaymgrReconcileError / OverlayMgrAPIRequestFailed / context deadline exceeded. OverlayManager pod CPU usage abnormally high (~60%). Another AKS cluster on same underlay generating excessive operations (noisy neighbor).
rootCause: A noisy neighbor AKS cluster on the same underlay generates excessive API operations (often from misconfigured DiagnosticsSettings with thousands of LinkedNotificationHandler.POST per hour), overloading shared OverlayManager pods and causing CPU saturation that prevents overlay reconciliation.
solution: 1. Identify noisy neighbor CCP via OverlaymgrApiQos Kusto query filtering by RPTenant and ReconcileOverlayError. 2. Look up offending CCP in ASI. 3. AKS PG contacts offending customer. 4. Alternative: migrate cluster to different underlay (requires customer approval + IcM to AKS PG). 5. Reconcile affected cluster after mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/OverlaymgrReconcileError caused by noisy neighbouring

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or nodepool scaling fails with PodDrainFailure: Drain did not complete pods within 10m0s on vm. Check Pod Disruption Budget. Upgrades are slow and eventually fail.
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) with AllowedDisruptions=0 prevent pods from being evicted during node drain. Occurs when PDB minAvailable threshold cannot be satisfied given current replica count and cluster topology.
solution: Option 1: Delete blocking PDB temporarily (backup with kubectl get pdb -o yaml, delete, upgrade, restore). Option 2: Scale up deployment/statefulset replicas so PDB allows disruptions, upgrade, then scale back. After mitigation, restart upgrade if not in-progress.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Pod Disruption Budgets cause nodepool operations to fail

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade, scaling, nodepool create/delete fails with ScopeLocked error: The scope cannot perform delete operation because following scope(s) are locked. Error references MC_ resource group.
rootCause: A resource lock (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) is present on the MC_ managed resource group or individual resources within it. AKS lifecycle operations require modifying resources in MC_ RG, and the lock blocks these operations.
solution: Remove the resource lock from MC_ resource group or the specific locked resource via Azure Portal, CLI (az lock delete), or PowerShell. After removing the lock, re-run the failed operation. Advise customer not to place locks on MC_ managed resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Resource locks block cluster operations

symptom: AKS upgrade or scale fails with Unable to get log analytics workspace info. Resource ID: .../workspaces/... Detail: ResourceGroupNotFound (HTTP 404). Log Analytics Workspace or its RG no longer exists.
rootCause: Customer deleted the Log Analytics Workspace or its containing Resource Group without first disabling the monitoring (omsagent) addon on the AKS cluster. The AKS control plane still references the workspace and fails when it cannot find it.
solution: Mitigation 1 (within 14 days): Recover soft-deleted workspace using PowerShell New-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace with same name/RG/region. Mitigation 2 (after 14 days): Disable monitoring addon (az aks disable-addons -a monitoring). Mitigation 3: If disable-addons fails, recreate the missing workspace from Portal with same resource ID. Then retry the operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Upgrade or Scale failed due to Log Analytics Workspace not found

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or nodepool scaling fails with PodDrainFailure error: Drain did not complete pods within 10m0s on vm. Check Pod Disruption Budget. ScaleVMSSAgentPoolFailed error code.
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budget (PDB) with AllowedDisruptions=0 prevents pods from draining off nodes during upgrade. If deployment replica count <= minAvailable in PDB, draining a single node violates the PDB constraint.
solution: Option 1: Back up PDB (kubectl get pdb -n <ns> -o yaml > backup.yaml), delete PDB, complete upgrade, restore PDB. Option 2: Scale up deployment/statefulset replicas so PDB allows disruptions during drain. After upgrade, scale back. Restart upgrade via original workflow or Jarvis reconcile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Pod Disruption Budgets cause nodepool operations to fail

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade/scale/nodepool operations fail with ScopeLocked error: The scope cannot perform delete operation because following scope(s) are locked. ResourceOperationFailure with UpgradeFailed.
rootCause: A resource lock (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) exists on the MC_ node resource group or individual infrastructure resources, preventing AKS RP from modifying resources during operations.
solution: Remove the resource lock from the MC_ resource group or individual locked resources via Azure Portal, CLI (az lock delete), or PowerShell. Then retry the failed operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Resource locks block cluster operations

symptom: AKS update/upgrade operations fail with Unable to get log analytics workspace info. ResourceGroupNotFound or 404 error referencing the monitoring Log Analytics workspace.
rootCause: Customer deleted the Log Analytics Workspace or its resource group without first disabling the AKS monitoring addon (omsagent). AKS control plane tries to access the workspace during operations and fails.
solution: Mitigation 1 (deleted <14 days): Recover workspace using New-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace with same name/region/RG. Mitigation 2 (>14 days): Disable monitoring addon: az aks disable-addons -a monitoring. Mitigation 3: Recreate the missing LA workspace from Portal, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Upgrade or Scale failed due to Log Analytics Workspace not found

symptom: AKS PUT operations fail with OverlaymgrReconcileError (SubCode: OverlayMgrAPIRequestFailed, context deadline exceeded). Kyverno validating webhook 'validate.kyverno.svc-fail' returns 'no endpoints available for service kyverno-svc'. APIServer poststarthook priority-and-fairness-config-producer fails.
rootCause: Kyverno validating webhook configuration (kyverno-resource-validating-webhook-cfg) blocks kube-apiserver readiness. The webhook calls kyverno-svc which has no running endpoints, causing a deadlock that prevents overlaymgr from communicating with the CCP API server.
solution: 1) Remove readiness/liveness probes from kube-apiserver container. 2) Delete the 'kyverno-resource-validating-webhook-cfg' ValidatingWebhookConfiguration from the overlay cluster. 3) Verify APIService v1beta3.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io is created. 4) Use Geneva action to reconcile the cluster. 5) Wait for new API servers to come up healthy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/OverlaymgrReconcileError caused by kyverno webhook failures

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade or nodepool operations fail with PodDrainFailure error: 'ScaleVMSSAgentPoolFailed - Drain did not complete pods within 10m0s. Check Pod Disruption Budget.' Slow upgrades that eventually fail.
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) with AllowedDisruptions=0 prevent pods from draining off nodes pending upgrade. When replica count <= PDB minAvailable, draining a node would violate the PDB constraint.
solution: Option 1: Back up (kubectl get pdb -o yaml) and delete the blocking PDB, re-run upgrade, restore PDB after. Option 2 (production): Scale up deployment/statefulset replica count (kubectl scale deploy --replicas N) to allow disruptions, re-run upgrade, scale back.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Pod Disruption Budgets cause nodepool operations to fail

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade, scaling, or nodepool operations fail with ScopeLocked error: 'The scope cannot perform delete operation because following scope(s) are locked'. Affects CRUD on resources in MC_ resource group.
rootCause: A resource lock (CanNotDelete or ReadOnly) exists on the MC_ resource group or individual resources, preventing AKS from modifying or deleting resources during lifecycle operations.
solution: Remove the resource lock from the MC_ resource group or affected resources via Azure Portal/CLI/PowerShell. After the lock is removed, re-run the failed operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Resource locks block cluster operations

symptom: AKS update/upgrade fails: 'Unable to get log analytics workspace info. StatusCode=404 Code=ResourceGroupNotFound'. Occurs during cluster PUT operations when monitoring addon references deleted workspace.
rootCause: Customer deleted the Log Analytics Workspace or its Resource Group without first disabling the monitoring (omsagent) addon on the AKS cluster.
solution: Mitigation 1: If deleted <14 days ago, recover soft-deleted workspace (New-AzOperationalInsightsWorkspace). Mitigation 2: If >14 days, disable monitoring addon: 'az aks disable-addons -a monitoring'. Mitigation 3: If disable-addons fails too, recreate the Log Analytics Workspace from Portal, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Upgrade and Update/Upgrade or Scale failed due to Log Analytics Workspace not found

symptom: Azure Backup extension install on AKS hangs until timeout. Extension agent logs: 'Failed to copy chart content for mcr.microsoft.com/azurearck8s/extension-catalog - dial tcp: lookup mcr.microsoft.com: i/o timeout'. EgressBlocked warnings on nodes.
rootCause: AKS cluster cannot reach MCR endpoints due to restrictive NSGs, DNS issues, or NVA/firewall rules. Extension agent cannot download Helm chart from MCR.
solution: Allow outbound access to: mcr.microsoft.com:443, *.data.mcr.microsoft.com:443, mcr-0001.mcr-msedge.net:443. Verify with 'curl -I https://mcr.microsoft.com/v2/_catalog' from debug pod. Check ASI Kube Events for EgressBlocked. Also ensure NSG inbound allows azurebackup and azurecloud service tags.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/AKS azure backup troubleshooting

symptom: Azure Backup extension appears installed (extension-agent/operator pods running in kube-system), ASI shows succeeded, but no 'dataprotection-microsoft' namespace or pods created. Backup/restore operations cannot proceed.
rootCause: 1) Azure Policy in enforced mode blocks pods with admin role/rolebinding in dataprotection-microsoft namespace. 2) Insufficient node resources (needs 128/1280Mi CPU, 500/1000m memory) to schedule dataprotection pods.
solution: 1) Add 'dataprotection-microsoft' to Azure Policy exclusion list. 2) Scale out or scale up worker nodes. Verify: kubectl get pods -n dataprotection-microsoft.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/AKS azure backup troubleshooting

symptom: 'az dataprotection backup-instance update-msi-permissions' fails: NoRegisteredProviderFound for API version '2023-02-02-preview' for type 'managedClusters'.
rootCause: The dataprotection CLI extension bundles a vendored AKS SDK with an outdated preview API version not registered in the target region.
solution: Create IcM with debug output (--debug flag). After PG repackages the vendored SDK, customer runs 'az extension update --name dataprotection'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/AKS azure backup troubleshooting

symptom: AKS API calls fail after preview API version deprecated; Terraform/az cli/SDK using retired preview API versions (e.g. 2022-07-02-preview through 2023-08-02-preview)
rootCause: AKS deprecates preview API versions on a rolling schedule. Old Terraform/SDK versions continue calling retired preview APIs removed from RP manifest.
solution: Update tools to latest GA API version: az cli 'az upgrade' + 'az extension update --name aks-preview'; Terraform update AzureRM; SDK update to latest. Check activity logs with az monitor activity-log list --offset 30d to identify deprecated API callers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/API Deprecation

symptom: Node drain times out leaving node cordoned with NoSchedule taint; node stuck in Ready,SchedulingDisabled or NotReady/Unreachable; AKS reconciler cannot return node to ready
rootCause: Pod eviction during drain timed out. Node controller cordon adds NoSchedule taint at drain start, only removed on uncordon. Timeout leaves taint permanently.
solution: kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data then kubectl uncordon <node>. For automation: nodify, draino, drainsafe tools. AKS platform built-in safe drain with node-problem-detector also available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Automatic Safe Drain Alternatives (Ready,SchedulingDisabled   NodeNotReady  Unreachable)

symptom: CCP scheduler spreads pods aggressively across zones; unexpected scheduling behavior differs from upstream default; pods not placed on same-zone nodes with available resources
rootCause: Since March 2025, AKS CCP scheduler uses maxSkew=1 (down from 5) for topology.kubernetes.io/zone, aggressively spreading multi-replica deployments across zones.
solution: Specify TopologySpreadConstraints in deployment spec with desired maxSkew (e.g. maxSkew: 5) to override AKS CCP default. Verify via KubeScheduler logs for --config= and maxSkew entries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/CCP Scheduler

symptom: AKS cluster Failed with CannotSwitchBetweenAutoAndManualApproval; CreateOrUpdatePrivateEndpointFailed for kube-apiserver PE; PE in pending state; all node pools failed
rootCause: Customer manually deleted auto-created kube-apiserver PE and recreated it manually, causing auto/manual approval mode conflict. Manual PE stays pending and blocks reconciliation.
solution: Delete the pending-state PE, then reconcile cluster (az aks update). kube-apiserver PE will be auto-recreated. Never manually modify/delete kube-apiserver PE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/CannotSwitchBetweenAutoAndManualApproval

symptom: AKS with CAS shows wrong node count; fewer nodes registered than CAS target (e.g. MIN=60 but only 51); CAS logs show negative delta=-9
rootCause: VMSS instances failed bootstrap with CSE exit code (e.g. 50) and never registered to k8s API server. CAS still counts unregistered VMSS instances causing persistent mismatch.
solution: Identify failed VMSS instances. Reimage (Use Temp Disk for Ephemeral OS) or delete them, then reconcile cluster. See vmssCSE failures TSG for exit codes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Cluster Autoscaler/AKS_not_showing_correct_node_count_when_CAS_is_enabled

symptom: CAS not scaling down to MIN count; nodes not suitable for removal can reschedule 0 out of N pods; nodepool scales back up after brief scale-down
rootCause: calico-typha deployment uses hostPort binding each replica 1:1 to a node. Tigera operator auto-scales typha replicas to match node count (1 per node for 1-3 nodes). hostPort prevents pod relocation.
solution: Set CAS MIN COUNT >= 3 (matching calico-typha replica needs) or use manual scaling for fewer nodes. Expected behavior with hostPort deployments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Cluster Autoscaler/Cluster Autoscaler is not scaling down when using Calico typha Operator

symptom: AKS cluster autoscaler creates new VMSS instances but they get deleted within seconds in a loop. New node added, then deleted, cycle repeats. Pod pending due to resource unavailability.
rootCause: VMSS autoscale is enabled alongside AKS cluster autoscaler. VMSS autoscale has a max instance count lower than what cluster autoscaler is trying to scale to. VMSS deletes nodes exceeding its configured maximum, then cluster autoscaler creates new ones again.
solution: Disable VMSS autoscale: use 'az monitor autoscale delete --name <autoscale-name> --resource-group <MC_RG>' or disable via Portal (VMSS > Scaling > Disable autoscale). After disabling, cluster autoscaler will work as expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Cluster Autoscaler/New VMSS instances keep creating and deleting

symptom: AKS cluster has duplicate control planes (multiple CCPs). Symptoms include: duplicate control plane logs, inaccurate Container Insights metrics, cluster autoscaler may not function.
rootCause: Rapidly deleting and creating an AKS cluster with the same name in the same resource group and subscription causes race condition. Backend resources (CCP, Network) are not fully cleaned up before the new cluster is created, resulting in two control plane instances.
solution: Escalate to AKS PG via IcM mentioning duplicate control planes for the cluster. No customer or CSS action can resolve this. PG will delete the duplicate CCP. Advise customer to add delay between DELETE and CREATE operations. Verify via AppLens detector or ASI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Duplicate cluster control planes

symptom: AKS API returns BadRequest: If-None-Match header is not empty or * when customer sends ETag request with invalid If-None-Match header value.
rootCause: Client sends If-None-Match header with a value other than * or empty. AKS pre-validation rejects the request. Only * or empty is accepted for If-None-Match.
solution: Client error. Customer should only pass * or leave If-None-Match header empty. Use FrontEndContextActivity Kusto query to verify.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: AKS API returns BadRequest: If-Match header is not empty and If-None-Match header is * - both ETag headers set simultaneously.
rootCause: Client sends both If-Match and If-None-Match headers in the same request. AKS pre-validation rejects this as a conflict.
solution: Client error. Customer must use only one of If-Match or If-None-Match per request, not both.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: AKS API returns PreConditionFailed: If-None-Match precondition failed. The given resource already exists.
rootCause: If-None-Match: * means only succeed if resource does not exist. Since the resource already exists, the precondition fails. This is by design for concurrency protection.
solution: Client error. Customer needs to use GET to check if resource exists first, or use If-Match with the current ETag value for update operations instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: AKS API returns PreConditionFailed: The value of If-Match header does not match the latest ETag value of the resource. Stale ETag.
rootCause: The resource was updated by another request between when the customer read the ETag and when they submitted their update. The If-Match ETag value is now stale.
solution: Client error. Customer must do a fresh GET to obtain the latest ETag, then retry the update with the new ETag value. Check FrontEndContextActivity Kusto query with pre-validating ETag headers message.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Entity Tag (ETag) Support

symptom: After migrating AKS KMS from v1 to v2, kubectl get secrets returns unable to transform key: no matching prefix found; reinitializing error. Secrets cannot be listed.
rootCause: Customer did not complete storage migration after KMS v1 to v2 migration. Secrets stored in etcd still have v1 prefix (k8s:enc:kms:v1:azurekmsprovider0) but API server expects v2 prefix. For clusters 1.27+, KMS must be disabled before upgrade and re-enabled after.
solution: Escalate to AKS PG via IcM. PG will: 1) access underlay via hcpdebug, 2) list secrets in etcd to find v1-prefixed entries, 3) edit encryption-provider-config configmap to change apiVersion from v2 back to v1, 4) restart kube-apiserver, 5) re-encrypt affected secrets, 6) verify recovery. CSS cannot resolve without PG engagement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/KMS list secrets error after migration

symptom: Customer requests proactive or standby support for AKS cluster upgrade, certificate rotation, or other planned maintenance operations
rootCause: AKS does not support proactive/standby scenarios through Microsoft Support. This is by design per AKS support policy.
solution: Inform customer that proactive/standby support is not available for AKS. For eligible customers, direct them to Azure Event Management (AEM) paid service which must be set up in advance. Customer should raise a case of correct severity if they encounter an issue during operations. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/support-policies
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/Additional Case Handling Processes/Break and Fix definition

symptom: Customer requests troubleshooting help for third-party software, OSS (e.g. Dremio, non-addon Istio, Nginx Ingress Controller), customer application code, or unsupported services (AKS Engine, vanilla k8s, on-prem k8s) running on AKS
rootCause: Third-party software, OSS, customer application code, and unsupported services are outside AKS support scope. Microsoft provides best-effort support only when third-party OSS integrates with Azure cloud provider.
solution: First verify AKS platform components are healthy. For supported integrations with Azure cloud provider, provide best-effort support using Microsoft documentation examples. For pure third-party/OSS issues, explain support boundaries and direct customer to vendor. Best-effort rules: single engineer/region, no 24x7/FTS/handovers, no guarantee of resolution. References: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/support-policies and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/general/support-policy-containers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/Additional Case Handling Processes/Break and Fix definition

symptom: Customer requests Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for a past AKS incident or outage, especially when node or logs have been deleted
rootCause: RCA is performed on best-efforts basis without SLA guarantees. If nodes/logs were deleted, investigation capability is severely limited.
solution: Handle RCA requests at Severity B/C (not Sev A). Do not hand over across regions except in rare political situations. Set clear expectation that RCA is best-effort with no guarantee. If logs are unavailable, explain that investigation is limited by available data. Use professional tone when communicating limitations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/Additional Case Handling Processes/Break and Fix definition

symptom: After upgrading AKS cluster (e.g. 1.18.14 -> 1.26.10 -> 1.28.5), Istio operator pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff, Istio ingress/egress not working, applications fail to deploy
rootCause: Older version of Istio Helm chart incompatible with the upgraded AKS/Kubernetes version
solution: Upgrade Istio to a version compatible with the current AKS version. Check Istio pod logs (kubectl logs) for specific errors. Note: Istio is a third-party application outside Azure support scope; direct customer to Istio community for in-depth Istio-specific support
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Best Practices For Customer Calls/Sample Scenario For Setting Right Expectations

symptom: Customer reports AKS cluster in failed state; ASC shows (deleted) next to cluster name, indicating the cluster was deleted before support investigation
rootCause: Customer deleted the cluster before support could investigate the reported issue
solution: Check AppLens for pre-deletion logs. Inform customer cluster was deleted and cannot be recovered. If issue recurs with new cluster, request: cluster name, resource group, subscription ID, reproduction steps (Portal/CLI/PowerShell/CloudShell), error details, screenshots, and business impact.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/Customer Deletes Cluster

symptom: Customer requests information about which user performed a specific operation such as deleting an Azure resource or Kubernetes component
solution: Customer own Support Data can be shared per Risk Management guidelines. Direct to Activity Logs (Azure Portal) for ARM operations or kube-audit logs (Log Analytics) for K8s operations. Refs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/monitor-aks-reference#activity-log, https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/monitor-aks#sample-log-queries
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/Request for Actions on Customer Side

symptom: Customer asks support engineer to access portal or make changes in their environment
solution: Decline per Microsoft security policy: We do not access customer data/applications nor make changes on customer side. These policies protect customer from potential issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/Accessing Customer Environment

symptom: Customer needs assistance configuring AKS environment with scripts but issue exceeds break-fix scope
solution: Premier customers: recommend CSA engagement through CSAM (check with account team first). Broad Commercial: refer to Azure Partners (https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/partners/).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/Configuration Script Writing Assistance

symptom: Customer asks about ETA for new AKS feature or Kubernetes version GA release
solution: If timeframe available: Product team targets [date], timeline not guaranteed and subject to change. If no timeframe: No ETA available, once provided timeline will not be guaranteed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/ETA Request For Feature or Version

symptom: Customer running deprecated/unsupported AKS version (outside N-2 full support or N-3 platform support) encounters issues such as Windows node deadlock or other known bugs fixed in newer versions
rootCause: Cluster is on an unsupported Kubernetes version; known bugs (e.g., containerd deadlock in v1.26) are fixed in newer AKS releases but not backported to EOL versions
solution: Attempt FQR and basic troubleshooting first; if issue is fixed in newer version, ask customer to upgrade using az aks upgrade --name <cluster> --resource-group <rg> --kubernetes-version <version>; check supported versions with az aks get-versions --location <region> --output table; provide AKS version support policy link; offer alternatives like porting to Linux nodepool if upgrade not immediately actionable
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Case Handling E2E/On Going Cases/Example Scenario Based Responses/Unsupported Version of AKS

symptom: CustomerNotifyWait error in Jarvis Actions when attempting AKS LockBox CustomerData access; JIT request alone insufficient to unlock live AKS object logs
rootCause: Customer subscription has Customer Lockbox for Microsoft Azure enabled, requiring explicit customer consent before Microsoft engineers can access customer data
solution: Contact customer to approve the pending Lockbox request via Azure Portal > Customer Lockbox > Pending Requests. If customer declines consent, troubleshoot via screensharing or ask customer to attach needed information to the support request. Note: only Global admins can enable/disable Customer Lockbox; request expires after 4 days; access granted for 8 hours default.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Customer Lockbox for Microsoft Azure - (JIT RequestApproval + Cx Approval) required

symptom: DfM fails to retrieve entitlements for TPID; case severity defaults to C and cannot be changed; error banner displayed when attempting severity change
rootCause: DfM entitlement retrieval failure for the case, potentially due to missing or incorrect entitlement association
solution: Copy subscription ID from case details > click dropdown arrow next to Edit SAP > select Edit Entitlement > paste subscription ID and search > select appropriate entitlement from results > Save
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Fix Incorrect Case Entitlement

symptom: Engineer cannot see customer data in ASC (Azure Support Center), message shows 'This Section may contain sensitive data'
rootCause: Customer declined to provide consent for advanced diagnostic data collection when creating the support case
solution: 1) Click 'Learn More' in ASC to get email template, ask customer to provide consent via Azure Portal > Support Request > Allow collection of advanced diagnostic information; 2) If customer refuses consent, troubleshoot via screensharing or ask customer to attach needed info
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Protecting Customer Data in ASC Logs Access Consent

symptom: Case created outside Azure Portal (phone, Partner portal, Dynamics), customer data not visible in ASC
rootCause: Cases created outside the Azure Portal do not trigger the data consent workflow, so customer data is not available in ASC
solution: 1) Troubleshoot manually using screensharing or file attachments; 2) Ask customer to create a new support request in Azure Portal using the email template from 'Learn More' link
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Protecting Customer Data in ASC Logs Access Consent

symptom: Customer submits AKS support case for Deployments, Namespaces, containerizing applications, or complex advisory scenarios
rootCause: These scenarios are outside AKS support scope. AKS support covers: API server connectivity, VM registration, horizontal scaling, version upgrades only
solution: Inform customer that AKS support scope covers: 1) API server connectivity; 2) VM cluster registration; 3) Horizontal scaling; 4) Minor/patch version upgrades. For Deployments/Namespaces/containerizing/advisory, refer to Kubernetes community. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-US/troubleshoot/azure/general/support-policy-containers
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Support Scope

symptom: Customer reports Microsoft.Rest.Azure.CloudException errors when using Azure Libraries for .NET to deploy AKS/ACI resources
rootCause: Deployment errors from Azure fluent/.NET libraries are not supported by Containers team; these are library-level issues
solution: If unable to reproduce via portal or CLI, direct customer to GitHub: https://github.com/Azure/azure-libraries-for-net
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Support Scope

symptom: Customer attempts to move AKS agent pool resource group and the operation fails
rootCause: AKS cluster does not support resource move operations on the agent pool (MC_) resource group
solution: Inform customer that AKS does not support moving the agent pool resource group. Customer needs to recreate the cluster in the target resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Support Scope

symptom: Need to share sample/script code with customer and ensure compliance with Microsoft legal requirements
solution: Include the standard Sample Code Disclaimer when sharing any code/scripts. Full disclaimer text available in wiki. Reference: OneStop Sample Code Policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Case Management/Sample Code Disclaimer

symptom: Engineer wants to raise ICM for AKS RP issue but unsure about approval process
rootCause: AKS RP ICMs require EEE team review before submission to avoid noise ICMs and ensure quality
solution: 1) Do NOT open ICM for AKS RP without going through EEE; 2) Consult SME/TA on Teams or Ava first; 3) Get TA approval in Teams channel; 4) Exception: weekends, can open directly if no TA available; 5) Ensure correct SAP; 6) Open from ASC; 7) Sev 2 ICM for RCA needs strong business impact + TA approval
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/ICM Quality Improvement/Improving Quality raising of ICMs

symptom: JIT access request rejected when requesting AKS JIT via aka.ms/aksjit on SAW machine for Jarvis/Geneva Actions
rootCause: Engineer is not a member of required OneIdentity groups AME\TM-AZCTS-AKS and AME\TM-AZCTS, or not a member of CoreIdentity entitlement WA CTS -14817
solution: 1) Join CoreIdentity entitlement WA CTS-14817 at https://coreidentity.microsoft.com (Corp-joined + Azure VPN). 2) Join OneIdentity groups AME\TM-AZCTS-AKS and AME\TM-AZCTS at https://aka.ms/oneidentity (SAW only). 3) Verify claims at https://tokendiagnosticprod.azurewebsites.net/ - check for Group claim REDMOND\RRD-WACTSRG-16492. 4) If still failing, contact jitsupport@ or file IcM to Azure Security Engineering JIT (ServiceId 10042, TeamId 34736)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Internal Operations/Requesting JIT

symptom: JIT Get Access window remains stuck in processing state when trying to access different Jarvis operations for AKS troubleshooting
rootCause: Known issue with JIT Get Access UI when switching between different Jarvis operations after JIT approval
solution: Workaround TBD (known issue). If JIT was approved but Get Access still fails, contact jitsupport@ or file IcM to Azure Security Engineering JIT on-call (ServiceId 10042, TeamId 34736). Note: only submit Get Access request once as it requires manual approval.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Internal Operations/Requesting JIT

symptom: After Microsoft alias change, engineer loses access to previously handled support cases in ASC (Azure Support Center) and related systems (DfM, VDM, WfM)
rootCause: ASC and related systems do not automatically update access permissions after alias changes; known system limitation requiring manual intervention
solution: 1) ASC cases: assign case to a peer then re-assign back to new alias. 2) Re-assign Yubikey at https://cloudmfa.support.windows.net/SecurityKeyServices/AssignSecurityKey and re-setup. 3) Rejoin CloudEnterprise and CloudEnterpriseAME silos. 4) Have direct manager update DfM, VDM, and WfM access for new alias.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Internal Operations/Post Alias Change Actions

symptom: After Kubernetes version upgrade, customer cannot connect to application through nginx ingress controller. Ingress stops routing traffic post-upgrade.
rootCause: Kubernetes version upgrade breaks compatibility with nginx ingress controller (and other cluster add-ons like LinkerD, akv2k8s). Nginx ingress has a supported versions matrix that must be matched to the K8s version.
solution: Check nginx ingress supported versions table (https://github.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx#supported-versions-table) and upgrade nginx ingress to a version compatible with the new K8s version. Similarly check compatibility for all other workloads/add-ons deployed on the cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Processes/Strategic Customer Processes/AT&T Reminders and Guidelines

symptom: AKS cluster cannot add new nodes after user modified VMSS extensions. Node provisioning fails because required components are missing from the VM.
rootCause: User changed the VMSS extension at VM level, which is not supported per Azure documentation. Custom VM extensions on AKS node pools can break required node components.
solution: Use AKSNodeInstaller DaemonSet to reinstall required components on affected nodes. Project: https://github.com/patnaikshekhar/AKSNodeInstaller. Note: nsenter behavior changed in Ubuntu 18.04 - update runOnHost.sh from /usr/bin/nsenter -m/proc/1/ns/mnt to /usr/bin/nsenter -n -m/proc/1/ns/mnt.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/AKS Cheatsheet

symptom: Intermittent networking issues in AKS pods that are difficult to reproduce. Need continuous packet capture without filling OS disk.
solution: Deploy k8s-tcp-dump-sidecar as a sidecar container for 24-hour rolling TCP dump traces, sending captures to Azure Files. This avoids filling the OS disk with continuous captures. Project: https://github.com/gjlumsden/k8s-tcp-dump-sidecar
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/AKS Cheatsheet

symptom: Need to capture network traces across multiple AKS Linux nodes simultaneously when application has many replicas spread across nodes.
solution: Use dspcap tool to capture network traces on all Linux nodes at once. Project: https://github.com/tdihp/dspcap. Useful when customer application has many replicas on multiple nodes and individual node captures are impractical.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/AKS Cheatsheet

symptom: AKS network routing issues caused by CIDR overlap between pod/node CIDRs and BGP Gateway routes or UDR routes in the VNET. Traffic may be dropped or misrouted when too many routes exist in customer environment.
rootCause: Conflicting routes pushed to VNET via BGP Gateway overlap with Pod or Node CIDRs. VNET also adds default routes with None nextHopType to drop traffic in certain scenarios. VNETs connected via Azure Virtual Network Manager may show unexpected ConnectedGroup nextHopType.
solution: Use cidroverlap tool to identify overlapping CIDRs: 1) Export NIC Effective Routes from ASC to Excel after running Test Traffic. 2) Run cidroverlap <target-CIDR> <excel-file> to find all overlapping routes with row numbers. Download: https://github.com/kennethgp/cidroverlap/releases. Use #.#.#.# format for single target IP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/CIDR overlap

symptom: After stopping and restarting a private AKS cluster via Azure Automation runbook, the cluster becomes inaccessible due to broken private DNS zone link to the virtual network
rootCause: Azure Automation Account configured at subscription level stops all AKS clusters including private ones. Stopping a private AKS cluster can break the private DNS zone link to the virtual network, causing DNS resolution failures for the API server FQDN
solution: Exclude private AKS clusters from the automation schedule by adding filtering logic in the runbook. If already affected, re-link the private DNS zone to the virtual network per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/private-clusters
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/Miscellaneous/Resource Shutdown Automation

symptom: When trying to send or reply to email from DFM (Dynamics 365), receive Access Is Denied error (Error code: 0x80048306). The error prevents outgoing emails from being sent.
rootCause: DFM email permission issue when replying directly with existing recipients in To/CC fields.
solution: Before clicking Send, copy all email addresses from the To and CC sections, delete them, then re-add them. This clears the permission error and allows the email to be sent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/Support/DfM and Case Buddy/Workaround for DFM outgoing email Access is Denied error

symptom: X509 certificate authentication error on SAW machine when using AME card - incorrect cert path prevents trust chain creation
rootCause: The AME certificate has an incorrect path that does not create the trust chain. The intermediate CA certificate (AME SC CA 01) is missing from the local certificate store.
solution: Download and install the certificate chain: open cert > Details > Authority Information Access > copy URL > download and install. Or run PowerShell (non-admin): curl "http://crl.microsoft.com/pkiinfra/Certs/BY2PKISCCA01.AME.GBL_AME SC CA 01(3).crt" -OutFile c:\windows	emp y2SC.cer; Import-Certificate -CertStoreLocation cert:\CurrentUser\CA\ -FilePath c:\windows	emp y2SC.cer
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/Support/SAW and CorpVM/AME Account Access Overview/Common Issues/AME Error when using X509 cert authentication on SAW

symptom: JIT access request immediately rejected: "The default JIT policy has been applied, request rejected as no elevation policy is in place"
rootCause: AME.gbl account is not assigned to the AME\TM-AZCTS-AKS security group, which became mandatory since July 15, 2020 for elevated JIT access.
solution: Join TM-AZCTS-AKS security group: SAW > Edge > https://oneidentity.core.windows.net > sign in with AME cert > Create Request > Security Group > domain=ame > search TM-AZCTS-AKS > add alias. Verify at https://jitaccess.security.core.windows.net/AuthInfo.aspx. If group already present, open ticket at aka.ms/cdocidmrequest.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACT Team/Tools/Support/SAW and CorpVM/AME Account Access Overview/Common Issues/Why was my JIT access request rejected

symptom: Azure Network Policy Manager (NPM) is being deprecated. NPM has scaling limitations (max 250 nodes), security gaps (cannot enforce NetworkPolicies on LoadBalancer/NodePort with externalTrafficPolicy=Cluster), and lacks FQDN/L7/cluster-wide policy features.
rootCause: NPM has fundamental scaling, security, and feature limitations. Upstream is transitioning to Cilium as default CNI for AKS.
solution: For Linux nodes: migrate to Cilium Network Policies (scales to 3000+ nodes). Use migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/migrate-from-npm-to-cilium-network-policy. For Windows nodes: switch to Calico or use NSGs. Linux NPM support ends Sept 2028, Windows NPM support ends Sept 2026.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/AKS Network Policy

symptom: NGINX Ingress controller (App Routing add-on) is being retired. Upstream Kubernetes deprecated NGINX ingress controller project with retirement date March 2026.
rootCause: Upstream Kubernetes NGINX ingress controller project end-of-life. No more security or bug fixes from upstream.
solution: Microsoft supports NGINX App Routing with critical security fixes until November 2026. Migration options: 1) App Routing Gateway API (meshless Istio) - Preview March 2026, GA May 2026. 2) Application Gateway for Containers - GA now, supports Ingress + Gateway API. 3) Istio add-on - Ingress GA, Gateway API Preview (GA targeted May 2026).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Application Routing (nginx) Add-on

symptom: Conflict when using Istio service mesh add-on and App Routing Gateway API Implementation simultaneously on AKS cluster.
rootCause: App Routing Gateway API deploys its own Istio control plane (non-revisioned, gatewayClassName: approuting-istio, controller: istio.aks.azure.com/gateway-controller) that conflicts with the Istio add-on (revisioned, gatewayClassName: istio, controller: istio.io/gateway-controller). They cannot coexist.
solution: Disable one before enabling the other. If migrating from Istio add-on to App Routing Gateway API: 1) Clean up all Istio CRDs and resources from prior installation. 2) Update gatewayClassName from 'istio' to 'approuting-istio'. 3) Remove revision labels (istio.io/rev=<asm-revision>). Verify via ASI or Kusto query that approuting-istio is enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Application Routing Gateway API (Istio) Add-on

symptom: Azure Policy assigned to AKS cluster but policies are not being enforced or applied. Constraint templates or gatekeeper not functioning.
rootCause: azure-policy pod in kube-system namespace or gatekeeper pods in gatekeeper-system namespace are not running, crashing, or throwing errors.
solution: 1) Verify azure-policy pod running: kubectl get pods -n kube-system. 2) Verify gatekeeper pod running: kubectl get pods -n gatekeeper-system. 3) Check logs for errors. For azure-policy pod issues: collab to Azure Policy team (Azure/Azure Policy/Policy behavior not as expected). For gatekeeper pod issues (crashing/restarting): ICM to Support/EEE AKS with gatekeeper audit and controller-manager pod logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Azure Policy/Azure Policy Overview

symptom: Azure Backup Extension fails to install on AKS cluster or dataprotection-microsoft pods are not running.
rootCause: Extension Manager not deployed correctly in AKS cluster, or Azure Backup deployment (dataprotection-microsoft) fails due to AKS platform issue or network connectivity issue.
solution: 1) Check if Extension Manager is deployed. 2) Check dataprotection-microsoft pods: kubectl get pods -n dataprotection-microsoft. 3) Check extension-agent and extension-operator pods in kube-system. 4) Follow Extension installation TSG (aka.ms/k8s-extensions-TSG). For extension issues: ICM to K8s Connect RP (Cluster Configuration/Cluster Configuration Triage). For backup-specific issues: ICM to ABRS using AKS Backup CRI Diagnostic Template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Azure backup extension

symptom: Container Insights tables (ContainerInventory, ContainerLog, InsightsMetrics, etc.) show no data or missing data in Log Analytics workspace.
rootCause: ama-logs pods (daemonset or replicaset) not running, or component failures in the data pipeline: Fluentd (container/K8s inventory), Fluent-bit (container logs), Telegraf (metrics), or MDSD (data export to LA). Also requires cluster to use managed identity (not service principal) - AMA only supports MSI for token/data send.
solution: 1) Verify ama-logs pods running (daemonset + deployment). 2) Confirm cluster uses managed identity, not service principal. 3) Check component logs by table: ContainerInventory->Fluentd (/var/opt/microsoft/docker-cimprov/log/fluentd.log), ContainerLog->Fluent-bit (/var/opt/microsoft/docker-cimprov/log/fluent-bit.log), InsightsMetrics->Telegraf (stderr), all->MDSD (/var/opt/microsoft/linuxmonagent/log/mdsd.*). 4) For Azure Managed Prometheus (ama-metrics pods): check ama-metrics daemonset and deployments in kube-system.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Container Insights

symptom: Cost Analysis data not appearing in Azure portal for AKS cluster. Cost Analysis scraper CronJob/Job failures.
rootCause: Cost Analysis addon requires standard or premium tier (not free) and managed identity. Scraper agent runs in cluster overlay, exports resource usage to AKS PAV2 EventHub -> CostManagement. Failures can be due to agent pod issues, HTTP 5xx errors, or EventHub export problems.
solution: 1) Verify addon active in ASI (may take 24h to sync). 2) Check CCP Pod Events for cost-analysis-* CronJobs. 3) Query CostAnalysis Kusto table for exported data. 4) Check error logs (data.level == 'error'). 5) For agent pod issues: check KubeSystemEvents. 6) If data exported successfully but not showing in portal: escalate to C+E CGI Cost Management/AIP-ACISTORE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Cost Analysis Addon

symptom: Customer attempts to remove server response headers using Envoy Lua filters in Azure Managed Istio, but the Server header is re-inserted by Envoy after Lua filter execution. Azure Managed Istio blocks advanced EnvoyFilter patches (e.g., ROUTE_CONFIGURATION).
rootCause: Envoy adds the Server header after Lua filters execute. Azure Managed Istio denylists EnvoyFilter and WasmPlugin resources, preventing customers from applying advanced configurations to strip the header.
solution: Create an IcM with PG to request whitelisting of envoy filters for the specific user subscription. Upon approval, PG can activate the Envoy filter toggle for the AKS cluster. Note: Envoy filters in Azure Managed Istio are not GA or officially supported; support is best-effort only.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Envoy_filters_Constraints_in_Azure_managed_Istio

symptom: KEDA operator logs show 'Failed to get API Group-Resources' with error 'Get https://10.0.0.1:443/api?timeout=32s: EOF'. KEDA metric server shows 'unable to run external metrics adapter' with same EOF error. KEDA pods fail to start.
rootCause: KEDA add-on cannot reach the Kubernetes API server due to misconfigured cluster firewall blocking required outbound network traffic.
solution: Configure the cluster firewall to allow required outbound traffic per AKS egress requirements: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/outbound-rules-control-egress
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/KEDA

symptom: KEDA addon is enabled (az aks show reports enabled=true), but keda-* pods are not present in kube-system namespace. Helm reconciliation error: 'rendered manifests contain a resource that already exists' with 'invalid ownership metadata' for CRDs like cloudeventsources.eventing.keda.sh.
rootCause: Leftover CRDs, ValidatingWebhookConfigurations, and other resources from a previous self-managed open-source KEDA installation conflict with the managed KEDA add-on Helm chart deployment. Multiple KEDA installations are not supported.
solution: Clean up all residual self-managed KEDA components: 1) Remove CRDs: 'kubectl get crd | grep keda' and delete them, 2) Remove ValidatingWebhookConfigurations: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep keda' and delete, 3) Disable KEDA add-on, 4) Re-enable KEDA add-on. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/keda-about#add-on-limitations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/KEDA

symptom: Customer receives error 'Kubernetes Dashboard addon is deprecated for Kubernetes version >= 1.19.0' (Code: AddonInvalid) when trying to enable the Kube Dashboard addon on AKS.
rootCause: Kubernetes Dashboard addon has been deprecated and removed for AKS clusters running Kubernetes version 1.19 or later.
solution: Direct customer to use the community-maintained Kubernetes Dashboard instead. Check current supported AKS addons at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/integrations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Kube Dashboard Addon

symptom: Open Service Mesh (OSM) addon is being retired and will be removed on 30 September 2027
rootCause: OSM is deprecated by Microsoft in favor of Istio service mesh
solution: Migrate to Istio following the migration guidance at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/open-service-mesh-istio-migration-guidance
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/Open Service Mesh

symptom: AKS Dapr sidecar fails to initialize with error: error creating CA: issuer chain does not belong to trust anchors: x509: certificate has expired
rootCause: Dapr mTLS self-signed CA certificate has expired. Default validity is only 1 year for Dapr self-signed certificates.
solution: Manually rotate CA certs per MS docs (Configure Dapr extension > Generate new self-signed cert). Note: rotation causes downtime. Recommend customers use their own certificates instead of Dapr self-signed certs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Addons and Extensions/dapr sidecar initialization failure

symptom: Error: Creating Windows node pools using ARM64 SKU is not supported
rootCause: ARM64 is not supported for Windows node pools in AKS
solution: Use Linux node pools for ARM64 workloads. Windows workloads must use non-ARM64 VM sizes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Arm64 node pools

symptom: Error: CVM enabled clusters are not supported for ARM64 enabled nodes when using ARM64 with CVM, Trusted Launch, or Pod Sandboxing
rootCause: ARM64 is incompatible with CVM, Trusted Launch, and Pod Sandboxing (Confidential Containers) in AKS
solution: Cannot combine ARM64 with these security features. Choose either ARM64 or CVM/Trusted Launch/Pod Sandboxing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Arm64 node pools

symptom: Error: Creating FIPS-enabled node pools using ARM64 SKU is not supported for non-Azure Linux V3 OS Skus
rootCause: FIPS on ARM64 is only supported with AzureLinux 3.0+ (Kubernetes 1.32+). Ubuntu and AzureLinux 2.0 are not supported.
solution: Use AzureLinux 3.0+ (k8s 1.32+) OS SKU for FIPS-enabled ARM64 node pools.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Arm64 node pools

symptom: Unrecognized arguments --crg-id error when creating AKS cluster or nodepool with Capacity Reservation Group
rootCause: aks-preview CLI extension version is outdated and does not support --crg-id parameter
solution: Run: az extension update --name aks-preview to upgrade to the latest version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Capacity Reservation Groups

symptom: LinkedAuthorizationFailed error when creating AKS cluster/nodepool with CRG: client does not have permission to perform write on linked scope
rootCause: AzureContainerService app/identity does not have permission to write to the Capacity Reservation Group resource group
solution: Assign the AKS service principal/managed identity write permission (Contributor role) on the CRG resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Capacity Reservation Groups

symptom: CreateVMSSAgentPoolFailed with BadRequest: Id is not a valid resource reference when using Capacity Reservation Group
rootCause: Customer provided incorrect CRG resource ID (e.g., pointing to capacityReservation instead of the group, or invalid format)
solution: Verify CRG resource ID is correct and points to the capacityReservationGroup, not individual reservations. Check CR inside CRG is in good state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Capacity Reservation Groups

symptom: Azure Portal does not allow AKS cluster to scale past 1000 nodes even though the 5000 node limit has been increased via ICM
rootCause: Portal UI has a hardcoded 1000 node limit that has not been updated
solution: Use Azure CLI to scale the cluster past 1000 nodes. Portal will be updated in the future.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: AKS node pool goes into failed state or takes very long when scaling by 200+ nodes, though all nodes are Ready in the cluster
rootCause: Internal bug: VMSS instance update for Application Security Groups is slow when processing large batches
solution: Scale up in smaller batches instead of large increments (avoid 200+ nodes at once).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: 429 TooManyRequests / OperationNotAllowed errors during AKS scale-up, cluster creation, or upgrades; cluster autoscaler unable to scale
rootCause: Azure API write/read throttling limits exceeded (e.g., HighCostGetVMScaleSet30Min quota)
solution: Run ARM throttling Kusto query in armprodgbl to identify throttled API requests by operationName/userAgent. File ICM to AKS/RP queue with query results.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: Sporadic egress connectivity failures on AKS clusters with >500 nodes due to SNAT port exhaustion
rootCause: SNAT port exhaustion from high outbound connection count at large scale
solution: Use AKS Managed NAT Gateway add-on (supports up to 64,512 flows per IP, max 16 IPs). See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/nat-gateway
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: Internal load balancer service creation is slow or fails with timeout on AKS clusters with >750 nodes
rootCause: Known performance bottleneck in AKS for service/ILB creation at large node scale
solution: Scale down to <750 nodes, create internal LB service (type LoadBalancer with azure-load-balancer-internal annotation), then scale back up. Or create ILB at cluster creation before scaling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/5k Node Limit

symptom: Kaito gpu-provisioner controller pod in failed state with panic: Configure azure client fails. Please ensure federated credential has been created for identity
rootCause: Federated credential has not been created for the Kaito addon identity ai-toolchain-operator-CLUSTER_NAME
solution: Create federated credential and role assignment for the identity. Verify with: az identity federated-credential list --identity-name ai-toolchain-operator-$CLUSTER_NAME -g $RESOURCE_GROUP
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AI Toolchain Operator (Kaito)

symptom: Kaito workspace RESOURCEREADY condition not true after 10+ minutes; GPU node never created
rootCause: Possible causes: (1) Identity lacks Contributor role on resource group, (2) Subscription lacks GPU SKU quota, (3) GPU instance type unavailable in region
solution: Check machine CR status for errors. Verify role assignment: az role assignment list --scope /subscriptions/$SUB/resourceGroups/$RG. Request quota increase if needed. Check GPU SKU regional availability.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AI Toolchain Operator (Kaito)

symptom: Kaito workspace INFERENCEREADY condition not true; inference pod starts but never becomes ready or cannot pull image
rootCause: Image pull failure (private mode: invalid URL or pull secrets), or inference image issue. Note: preset images are 30-100GB, expect long pull times.
solution: For image pull errors with private images: validate image URL and pull secrets. For preset model issues: contact Kaito dev team (kaito-dev@microsoft.com). For custom images: customer responsibility.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AI Toolchain Operator (Kaito)

symptom: Error when updating existing AKS cluster to Automatic SKU because cluster SKU tier is not standard
rootCause: AKS Automatic SKU requires Standard tier as prerequisite
solution: Run: az aks update -g <resource-group> -n <cluster-name> --tier standard before converting to Automatic.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS Automatic SKU

symptom: Multiple validation errors when updating existing AKS cluster to Automatic SKU: missing required features/addons (azure-policy, apiserver-vnet-integration, image-cleaner, safeguards, auto-upgrade, NAT gateway, web-app-routing, keyvault-secrets-provider, workload-identity, CNI overlay with Cilium, VPA, KEDA, NRG lockdown, node-provisioning, availability-zones, ephemeral-os-disks, node-os-upgrade, disable-ssh, system-pool-taint, azure-rbac, disable-local-accounts)
rootCause: AKS Automatic requires 20+ features/addons to be enabled. Some (AvailabilityZones, Web App Routing, Ephemeral OS Disks) must be enabled at cluster creation time.
solution: Enable each required feature using specific az aks CLI commands. Key creation-time requirements: --zones 1 2 3, --enable-app-routing, --node-osdisk-type Ephemeral. See ADO wiki for full list of CLI commands per error message.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS Automatic SKU

symptom: AKS operations fail with 'failed calling webhook' errors; upgrade operations show 'broken pipe' in API server logs when calling kyverno or other webhook endpoints; pod creation/scaling fails with 'context deadline exceeded' on webhook admission endpoints
rootCause: Connectivity between API server instances and webhook handler pods is disrupted; konnectivity-agent tunnel is unhealthy or webhook pods are unavailable to handle incoming admission requests
solution: Immediate mitigation: kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfiguration <webhook-name> or kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration <webhook-name>. Then investigate konnectivity-agent tunnel health and check webhook pod status for readiness
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Kubernetes admission webhook related TSG

symptom: FluxConfiguration resource is stuck in Creating state or changes are not being applied; kubectl get fluxconfiguration shows the resource is not progressing
rootCause: fluxconfigs.clusterconfig.azure.com CRD does not exist on the cluster; typically caused by failed or incomplete Flux extension installation
solution: Re-issue PUT request on the microsoft.flux extension to reinitialize the helm install loop and re-provision the CRD. If re-issuing PUT fails, delete the current microsoft.flux extension and reinstall it
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: FluxConfiguration changes not reflecting on cluster; configuration applying locally but stuck in Creating state; Kusto shows 'status not yet updated with the latest generation' for FluxConfigAgent
rootCause: FluxConfigAgent cannot communicate with the Azure dataplane to post status back; caused by invalid/missing identity token, network blocking HTTPS to DP endpoint, or HIS certificate retrieval failure
solution: Check fluxconfig-agent pod logs for communication errors; verify HTTPS connectivity from pod to <location>.dp.kubernetesconfiguration.azure.com; query Kusto ConfigAgentTraces for 401/403 responses or 'Failed to get certificate from HIS' errors; verify connectedCluster resource exists in ARM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: Flux GitRepository shows not ready; FluxConfiguration not syncing or applying Git changes to cluster; kustomization resources not being created
rootCause: GitRepository CR does not exist; auth secret missing or contains invalid credentials (wrong SSH private key, missing known_hosts for private repo, invalid branch name)
solution: Verify GitRepository CR: kubectl get gitrepository -n <namespace> <config-name> -o yaml; check auth secret contains valid base64 credentials (username/password for HTTPS or identity/identity.pub/known_hosts for SSH); verify branch name matches actual repo branch
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: Flux extension installation or uninstallation fails with AuthorizationFailed error: 'does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.ContainerService/managedclusters/extensionaddons/read'
rootCause: Customer subscription not registered to required resource providers: Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration, Microsoft.Kubernetes, and/or Microsoft.ContainerService
solution: Register RPs: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration --wait; az provider register --namespace Microsoft.Kubernetes --wait; az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService --wait. Verify with az provider show -n <namespace> -o table
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Microsoft.Flux FluxConfiguration TSG

symptom: ARM template deployment fails with BadRequest code NotAllAgentPoolOrchestratorVersionSpecifiedAndUnchanged when upgrading a single nodepool using Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters resource type
rootCause: The managedClusters ARM resource type requires all agent pools to be consistently specified; 2022-03-27 API behavioral change prevents implicit nodepool upgrades via cluster-level API
solution: Use Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/agentPools resource type in ARM template for per-nodepool upgrades (equivalent to az aks nodepool upgrade CLI command)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/NotAllAgentPoolOrchestratorVersionSpecifiedAndUnchanged error when upgrading a single nodepool using ARM

symptom: After upgrading to AKS 1.31+, workloads experience increased latency for container image pull operations or slower node startup compared to pre-1.31 behavior
rootCause: AKS 1.31 changed default image pull mode from serialized to parallel; parallel pulls can cause disk I/O contention in edge case workload patterns
solution: Use ephemeral OS disks; increase OS disk size; use newer/larger VM SKUs; or switch back to serialized image pulls (requires ICM to PG for configuration change)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Parallel Image Pulling

symptom: AKS node reports DiskPressure condition; kubelet logs show 'failed to garbage collect required amount of images. Wanted to free X bytes, but freed 0 bytes'; nodes flapping between Ready and NotReady
rootCause: Kubelet image garbage collection fails because it cannot determine the last-used timestamp for some images; images with unknown last-used times are not garbage collected even under disk pressure
solution: Access node via SSH or kubectl debug node <node-name> -it --image=mcr.microsoft.com/cbl-mariner/base/core:2.0; chroot /host; run 'crictl rmi --prune' to manually remove unused images; verify with df -h
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Pruning unused images from nodes

symptom: AKS cluster creation, node pool addition, scaling, start, or upgrade fails with quota exceeded error: 'Operation results in exceeding quota limits of Core' or 'would exceed the quota of N resources of type...per resource group'
rootCause: Subscription vCPU quota (Total Regional vCPUs or specific VM size family vCPUs) is insufficient for the requested AKS operation
solution: Submit quota increase via Azure portal (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/quotas/quickstart-increase-quota-portal). For large/high-demand SKUs requiring manual review, or if customer declines new request, transfer case to SAP path: Azure\Service and subscription limits (quotas)\ComputeVM (coresvCPUs) with: Subscription ID, Region, SKU Size, Restriction Type (Regional/Zonal), cores requested. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team for quota issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Quota errors

symptom: AKS cluster or node pool creation fails with 'SKU Not Found' or 'VM Size Not Available' error for the requested VM size
rootCause: Requested VM size is restricted at the subscription level (ASC > Resource Explorer > Subscription > RP Details > SKU Restrictions), or the VM size is not offered in the specific region/zone due to platform limitations
solution: Check SKU availability: `az vm list-skus --location <region> --size <vmsize> --all --output table`. If subscription-level restriction, create collaboration request to Azure\Service and subscription limits (quotas)\ComputeVM with: Subscription ID, Region, SKU Size, Restriction Type, cores requested. If platform limitation, advise customer to select alternative VM size or zone using Compute Capacity Advisor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/SKU Not Found OR VM Size Not Available

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails; ASC Extensions tab shows vmssCSE (custom script extension) failed with error code 50 or 'ERR_OUTBOUND_CONN_FAIL'
rootCause: Outbound connectivity from the new node is blocked by NSG rules or UDR (User Defined Routes) configuration, preventing the node from reaching required endpoints during provisioning
solution: Ask customer to review NSG and UDR rules to ensure required outbound connectivity is not blocked. Reference CSE error codes in AgentBaker cse_helpers.sh: https://github.com/Azure/AgentBaker/blob/master/parts/linux/cloud-init/artifacts/cse_helpers.sh
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/VMSS Overview

symptom: AKS GPU node provisioning fails; ASC Extensions tab shows vmssCSE failed with error code 85 or 'ERR_GPU_DRIVERS_INSTALL_TIMEOUT'
rootCause: GPU driver installation timed out during node provisioning; the installation may still be in progress and can eventually complete successfully
solution: Wait for GPU driver installation to complete - error code 85 indicates timeout but installation may succeed given enough time. Monitor node status; if it does not recover, reimage the node and allow sufficient time for GPU driver install.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/VMSS Overview

symptom: kubectl exec / docker exec fails with 'OCI runtime exec failed: exec failed: unable to start container process: open /dev/ptmx: no space left on device: unknown'; accessing pods or nodes with debug commands fails
rootCause: PTY (pseudo-terminal) pool exhausted on the node: /proc/sys/kernel/pty/nr has reached /proc/sys/kernel/pty/max (default 4096). Caused by processes or containers creating PTY sessions without closing them (PTY leak), often from third-party containers (e.g., aqua-agent)
