solution: (1) Temporarily increase PTY limit: `az vmss run-command invoke --resource-group <rg> --name <vmss> --command-id RunShellScript --instance-id <id> --scripts 'sysctl -w kernel.pty.max=8192'`. (2) Use node-shell to access the node. (3) Run `lsof > lsof.txt && ps axjf > ps.txt`. (4) Identify leaking processes: `grep -E '/ptmx|/pts/' lsof.txt | awk '{print $1, $2}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr`. (5) Map container IDs to pods with `crictl pods`. (6) If third-party pods are responsible, contact the vendor for a fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Virtual Machine TSGs/Node PTY

symptom: AKS nodes report NodeNotReady status despite no memory/disk/network/kubelet resource pressure. Node kubelet logs show: 'Failed to ensure lease exists, will retry' with error 'unable to create new content in namespace kube-node-lease because it is being terminated'
rootCause: The kube-node-lease namespace is stuck in Terminating state due to a finalizer (e.g., Rancher controller.cattle.io/namespace-auth) blocking namespace deletion. This prevents kubelet from updating its heartbeat lease, causing the control plane to mark nodes as NotReady.
solution: (1) Inspect namespace: `kubectl describe namespace kube-node-lease`. (2) Remove blocking finalizers: `kubectl patch namespace kube-node-lease -p '{"metadata":{"finalizers":[]}}' --type=merge`. (3) Verify namespace returns to Active status and nodes return to Ready. Note: kube-node-lease namespace is expected to remain Active in AKS; if accidentally deleted it is auto-recreated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Virtual Machine TSGs/NodeNotReady Kube node lease namespace

symptom: After scale-up with deallocate mode, AKS node is NotReady even though VMSS instance is running; pods show as running on the node; /var/lib/kubelet/kubeconfig not found on the node; node-problem-detector shows 'invalid kubernetes master url specified'; kubelet is not running
rootCause: Combination of kubelet_disk_type=Temporary + scale_down_mode=Deallocate + VM SKU supporting both OS cache disk and temporary storage (Dsv3, Esv3, M, FX-series). VM stop/start during scale-down/up causes temporary storage loss, deleting kubelet data including kubeconfig and containerd data.
solution: Short-term: Reimage the affected node. Long-term: Avoid combining kubelet_disk_type=Temporary and scale_down_mode=Deallocate on node pools using VM SKUs that support both OS cache disk and temporary storage (Dsv3/Esv3/M/FX-series). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/aks/managed-clusters/create-or-update?view=rest-aks-2024-10-01&tabs=HTTP#kubeletdisktype
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Virtual Machine TSGs/VM Node NotReady kubelet missing

symptom: Microsoft.ContainerService Resource Provider stuck in 'Registering' state for >30 minutes; 'az provider list' shows sustained Registering status preventing new cluster deployment
rootCause: RP failing to respond to registration request due to high load, internal errors, code changes, or ARM/RP regional issues; verifiable via ARMProd Kusto logs filtering by correlationID and register+containerservice keywords
solution: Unregister and restart the Resource Provider registration via Azure Portal. Use ARMProd Kusto queries (Requests/HttpOutgoingRequests, Traces/Errors tables) with correlationID and register+containerservice filters to diagnose root cause
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Resource Provider registration stuck

symptom: Cluster deletion fails with 'ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked' - MC_ resource group cannot be deleted because Microsoft.RecoveryServices vault keeps recreating itself during cluster deletion
rootCause: Azure Backup/ASR vault dependency on the MC_ resource group: vault auto-recreates during deletion, blocking the managed resource group from being removed
solution: Retry vault deletion manually; if vault continues recreating, open collab with ASR team (support area: Azure/Azure Backup/Vault delete and move) to remove vault dependency, then retry cluster deletion
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked: Vault Recreating During Cluster Deletion

symptom: Node pool creation fails with: OSSKU='Ubuntu2404' is invalid, details: Selected kubernetes version is not supported for Ubuntu2404, use at least 1.32.0
rootCause: Ubuntu2404 osSku requires minimum Kubernetes 1.32.0; using it with older k8s versions is not supported
solution: Upgrade cluster Kubernetes version to at least 1.32.0 before using Ubuntu2404 osSku, or use Ubuntu/Ubuntu2204 osSku for Kubernetes versions below 1.32
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Ubuntu

symptom: Node pool create/update fails with InvalidOSSKU: FIPS mode is not supported with ossku=Ubuntu2404 or Selected VM size is not supported with ossku=Ubuntu2404 (CVM+Ubuntu2404)
rootCause: Ubuntu2404 has feature compatibility restrictions: FIPS not supported (requires Ubuntu 22.04 or below); CVM+Ubuntu2404 requires k8s 1.33+; Ubuntu2404 cannot be combined with FIPS or certain Arm64/Trusted Launch+CVM configurations
solution: For FIPS workloads: use Ubuntu/Ubuntu2204 osSku (FIPS for Ubuntu2404 not yet supported). For CVM+Ubuntu2404: ensure k8s >= 1.33. Check OS SKU compatibility matrix before combining osSku with special features
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Cluster Management/Ubuntu

symptom: Pod fails to start on newly provisioned GPU node with: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: transport: Error while dialing: dial unix /run/containerd/containerd.sock: connect: connection refused - but recovers after first restart
rootCause: New GPU node transitions to ready/schedulable state before full initialization; pod (e.g. GitLab CI runner) is scheduled and attempts to pull container image before containerd runtime is ready, causing CrashLoopBackOff on first attempt
solution: 1) Taint GPU node pool and add tolerations/nodeSelectors for GPU-requiring pods only. 2) Add retry logic to CI job definitions. 3) Add pre-check script waiting for containerd socket availability. 4) Use DaemonSet to pre-pull large images on GPU node startup
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Containerd socket errors on GPU nodes

symptom: Node Problem Detector (NPD) reports GPU conditions (GpuCount) on AKS nodes; pods fail scheduling due to missing/incorrect GPU resource counts; nvidia-smi reports Failed to initialize NVML: Unknown Error or shows fewer GPUs than expected
rootCause: GPU driver issues (incorrect, corrupted, or outdated drivers preventing proper GPU detection), GPU hardware malfunction, or NPD misconfiguration leading to incorrect GPU condition detection/reporting
solution: 1) Identify affected nodes via KubeNodeStatusCondition Kusto query filtering by GpuCount condition. 2) Run nvidia-smi (or rocm-smi for AMD) on the node to check driver status. 3) Review /var/log/azure/cluster-provision.log and dmesg | grep -i nvidia for driver errors. 4) Reboot for transient issues; reimage/delete node for persistent driver/hardware problems
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/GPU Node Problem Detector

symptom: Windows pod sandbox creation fails with 'The user name or password is incorrect.: unknown' error after migrating from Docker to containerd runtime
rootCause: Docker ignored runAsUserName at pod-spec level when starting pause container, but containerd does not. containerd attempts to find the specified user inside the pause container image, causing authentication failure.
solution: Move securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName from pod-level spec to individual container-level spec. Do not set runAsUserName in pod.spec.securityContext; set it only in pod.spec.containers[].securityContext instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Docker Deprecation

symptom: Windows pod enters CrashLoopBackOff with error 'hcs::System::CreateProcess <deployment>: The system cannot find the file specified.: unknown' after migrating from Docker to containerd
rootCause: Dockerfile ENTRYPOINT uses backslash-relative path format (.\ScriptName) which Docker handled but containerd does not. containerd cannot resolve the backslash path correctly.
solution: Change ENTRYPOINT from shell form with backslash path to exec form using PowerShell: ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-Command", ".\\script.ps1"]. See https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/5067
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Docker Deprecation

symptom: ContainerRuntime remains containerd even when specifying --aks-custom-headers WindowsContainerRuntime=docker when creating Windows agent pools on Kubernetes v1.24+
rootCause: Docker is no longer supported as container runtime in AKS Windows agent pools for k8s v1.24+. The flag WindowsContainerRuntime=docker is silently ignored for v1.24+ Windows node pools.
solution: Use containerd as the container runtime on AKS Windows nodes with k8s v1.24+. Docker was fully removed from AKS Windows nodes as of September 2022. Migrate workloads to be containerd-compatible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Docker Deprecation

symptom: Running 'az aks upgrade' or 'az aks nodepool upgrade' does not upgrade existing Windows nodepools from Gen1 to Gen2 VM generation as expected
rootCause: By design, az aks upgrade / az aks nodepool upgrade only upgrades the node image version, not the VM Generation. Changing VM Generation requires creating a new nodepool.
solution: Create a new nodepool that supports Gen2 on the upgraded cluster: for k8s >= 1.25 specify a Gen2 VM size; for k8s < 1.25 specify --os-sku Windows2022 and a Gen2 VM size. Migrate workloads to the new nodepool and delete the old Gen1 nodepool.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Gen2 VMs

symptom: AKS Windows nodepool creation fails with: '<VM-SKU> is a Gen 2-only VM. Windows2019 does not support Gen 2 VMs. However, you can select a Gen 1 VM size or set os_sku to 2022.' (error code: ErrorCode_Windows2019NotSupportedWithGen2VM)
rootCause: Gen2 VM sizes are not supported on Windows2019 nodepools by design. Windows2019 only supports Gen1 VMs.
solution: Select a Gen1 VM size to use on Windows2019, or specify --os-sku Windows2022 to create a Windows2022 nodepool which supports Gen2 VM sizes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Gen2 VMs

symptom: After upgrading AKS cluster to k8s >= 1.25, Windows2019 application pods are rescheduled to Windows2022 Gen2 nodepool; node label kubernetes.azure.com/os-sku changes to Windows2022 unexpectedly
rootCause: Cluster upgrade reprovisions all nodes and refreshes all labels from scratch. Default Windows version for k8s >= 1.25 is Windows2022. Without explicit node selector for Windows2019, pods may be scheduled onto the new Windows2022 nodes.
solution: Before upgrading to k8s >= 1.25, add node affinity or selector to Windows2019 pods: Option A: Use affinity with NotIn operator for kubernetes.azure.com/os-sku: Windows2022. Option B: Recreate Windows2019 nodepools and add NodeSelector for Windows2019 os-sku. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/upgrade-windows-2019-2022
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Gen2 VMs

symptom: Customer's own windows-exporter or Windows service fails to start on AKS Windows node: 'listen tcp :19182: bind: Only one usage of each socket address (protocol/network address/port) is normally permitted.'
rootCause: AKS installs windows-exporter via AKSWindowsExtension using port 19182 (not the open-source default 9182). If a customer deployment also uses port 19182, a port conflict occurs.
solution: Configure the customer's windows-exporter to use a different port (e.g., 29182). Use Kusto query on ControlPlaneEvents kube-audit category to identify DaemonSets with hostPort 19182 conflicts. ICM ref: 530938116.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Node Extension

symptom: Pod fails with ImagePullBackOff or ErrImagePull on Windows2022 node: 'Back-off pulling image mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2019' or similar Windows2019-based container image
rootCause: Windows2022 nodes cannot host and run Windows2019 (ltsc2019) container images due to OS version incompatibility. Windows container images are strictly OS-version specific.
solution: Update the application container image from Windows2019 base (e.g., mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2019) to Windows2022 equivalent (mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022). Follow migration guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/upgrade-windows-2019-2022
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Retirements

symptom: AKS nodepool creation fails with: 'Cannot create Windows2022 node pools with the current Kubernetes version VERSION because Windows2022 was deprecated after Kubernetes version 1.34.0. Please create Windows2025 or Windows Annual Channel node pools instead.'
rootCause: AKS stopped supporting Windows2022 node pools from Kubernetes version 1.34.0 as part of the Windows2022 retirement plan (planned retirement: March 2027).
solution: Create Windows2025 or Windows Annual Channel node pools: --os-type Windows --os-sku Windows2025 or --os-sku WindowsAnnualChannel. Migrate workloads from Windows2022 to the new nodepools.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Retirements

symptom: AKS cluster upgrade fails with: 'Cannot upgrade to the target Kubernetes version VERSION because there are Windows2022 node pools deprecated after Kubernetes version 1.34.0. Please migrate workloads to Windows2025 or Windows Annual Channel and remove Windows2022 node pools before the upgrade.' Both control-plane-only and full upgrade are blocked.
rootCause: AKS blocks cluster upgrade to k8s >= 1.34.0 when Windows2022 nodepools exist, because Windows2022 is not supported from k8s 1.34.0 onwards.
solution: 1. Create new Windows2025 or WindowsAnnualChannel nodepools. 2. Migrate workloads from Windows2022. 3. Delete all Windows2022 nodepools. 4. Retry the cluster upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/Windows Retirements

symptom: After scaling up Windows nodepool with Standard_E32s_v5 SKU, nodes enter NotReady state; kubelet crashes with configMapVolumeMounter.SetUpAt() panic (invalid memory access 0xffffffffffffffff); pods mounting specific ConfigMap stuck in Terminating; CNS becomes unresponsive
rootCause: ConfigMap containing Windows-specific UNC file paths and PowerShell scripts triggers invalid memory access during volume mounting on Standard_E32s_v5. Likely caused by memory architecture differences in v5 series vs v4, creating incompatible access pattern when processing ConfigMap content.
solution: Modify the ConfigMap to remove or encode UNC file paths and embedded PowerShell scripts. Workaround: switch to Standard_E32s_v4 VMs. Escalate to AKS engineering (kubelet team) for a permanent fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Compute/Windows/kubelet crash different memory arch

symptom: CVM enabled node pools cannot be created with Arm64, Trusted Launch, FIPS, or Pod Sandboxing settings enabled
rootCause: Confidential VM (CVM) node pools have specific hardware/firmware requirements that are incompatible with Arm64 architecture, Trusted Launch, FIPS, and Pod Sandboxing features
solution: Remove the incompatible setting (Arm64/Trusted Launch/FIPS/Pod Sandboxing) before creating the CVM node pool. Use only supported AMD SEV-SNP compatible VM SKUs (DCav5/ECav5)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS Confidential VM (CVM)

symptom: InvalidOSSKU error when creating CVM node pool: OSSKU=Ubuntu2204 is invalid, osSKU Ubuntu2204 and Ubuntu2404 are not supported on CVM VM sizes
rootCause: Only specific OS versions support CVM: Ubuntu 20.04 (k8s 1.24-1.34), Ubuntu 24.04 (k8s 1.32-1.38), Azure Linux 3.0 (k8s 1.31+). Ubuntu 22.04, Azure Linux 2.0, and Windows are not supported
solution: Use a supported OS SKU for CVM node pools: Ubuntu2004 (k8s 1.24-1.34), Ubuntu2404 (k8s 1.32+), or AzureLinux3 (k8s 1.31+). Refer to the CVM supported OS versions compatibility table
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS Confidential VM (CVM)

symptom: Pod subnet full: nodes cannot request more IPs from delegated subnet, pods stuck in ContainerCreating. DNC logs show subnet capacity exceeded. Affects AKS clusters using Azure CNI dynamic pod IP allocation (SWIFT)
rootCause: The pod subnet CIDR is exhausted because IPs are allocated in batches of 16 per node (minimum 16, requesting another 16 when <8 free). Shared pod subnets across multiple clusters/nodepools accelerate exhaustion
solution: Reduce pod count if possible. Create a new pod subnet on the same VNet and create a new node pool using the new pod subnet. Use AppLens Pod Subnet Full detector or Resource Health dashboard to confirm. Check for subnet sharing with Kusto: AgentPoolSnapshot | where podSubnetId contains '<subnet-id>' | distinct clusterName, name
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/AKS dynamic pod IP allocation

symptom: High rate of HTTP 429 responses from API Server. aks-managed-apiserver-guard flowschema and prioritylevelconfiguration are automatically applied to the cluster, limiting client traffic
rootCause: API Server OOM killed event frequency exceeds 10 occurrences within 10 minutes. The overlaymgr/ccpautoscaler OOM watcher detects this and applies APF (API Priority and Fairness) throttling to protect the API server
solution: 1) Inform customer this is protective throttling. 2) Customer should identify and fix unoptimized API clients. 3) Remove the flowschema and prioritylevelconfiguration. 4) If overlaymgr keeps re-creating them, add label aks-managed-skip-update-operation:true to prevent reconciliation. 5) Increase prioritylevelconfiguration values to loosen limits
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/API Server Priority And Fairness Throttling

symptom: AKS does not natively support Application Security Groups (ASGs) in standard deployment. Customer wants to apply ASG rules to AKS node pools
rootCause: AKS does not natively integrate ASGs into its deployment process. ASGs are an Azure Virtual Network feature for grouping VM NICs, but AKS deployment does not include ASG configuration
solution: Option 1: Use Host IP path - configure node pool with public IPs and apply ASG via host-port connections. Option 2: After AKS provisioning, manually assign node NICs to desired ASGs. Option 3: Use cloud-native network security (Network Policies, Kata containers). See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-node-public-ips#allow-host-port-connections-and-add-node-pools-to-application-security-groups
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/ASG Support

symptom: After enabling addon-autoscaling, VPA recommends too large CPU/memory resources for AKS managed add-ons (CoreDNS, Workload Identity, etc.), causing pods to enter Pending state with FailedScheduling: Insufficient cpu/memory
rootCause: VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) recommended resource requests exceed available node capacity. The add-on pods cannot find a suitable node to schedule on
solution: 1) Enable cluster autoscaler to add more nodes, or create a new node pool with larger VM SKUs. 2) To disable VPA for a specific add-on: set annotation kubernetes.azure.com/override-update-mode: enabled and change updateMode to Off in the VPA CR. 3) To disable addon-autoscaling entirely: az aks update --name <cluster> --resource-group <rg> --disable-addon-autoscaling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Addon Autoscaling

symptom: Pod creation rejected: pods is forbidden: pod node label selector conflicts with its namespace node label selector. Or pods stuck Pending with FailedScheduling: node(s) did not match node selector. PodNodeSelector admission controller enabled on AKS >= 1.19
rootCause: PodNodeSelector admission controller enforces the namespace annotation scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/node-selector. If a pod specifies a conflicting nodeSelector it is rejected. If no nodes match the enforced label, pods remain Pending
solution: 1) Check namespace annotation: kubectl get ns <ns> -o yaml | grep node-selector. 2) Ensure pod nodeSelector matches or remove conflicting nodeSelector from pod spec. 3) Ensure nodes have the required label. 4) Remove the namespace annotation if unintended
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Admission Controllers

symptom: Pod creation rejected: pod tolerations (possibly merged with namespace default tolerations) conflict with its namespace allowlist. PodTolerationRestriction admission controller on AKS >= 1.19
rootCause: PodTolerationRestriction admission controller verifies pod tolerations against namespace toleration whitelist (annotation scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/tolerationsWhitelist). Pod default tolerations (not-ready, unreachable) must also be in the whitelist
solution: 1) Check namespace annotations for tolerationsWhitelist. 2) Ensure ALL required tolerations are in the whitelist, including default tolerations: node.kubernetes.io/not-ready:NoExecute and node.kubernetes.io/unreachable:NoExecute. 3) Add any custom tolerations used by pods to the whitelist
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Admission Controllers

symptom: Agentic CLI for AKS error: The combined size of system_prompt and user_prompt exceeds the maximum context size of 4096 tokens available for input
rootCause: GPT-4 model has a smaller context window (4096 tokens). The combined prompt exceeds this limit
solution: Switch to a model with a larger context window (e.g., GPT-4o, GPT-4-turbo) using the --model parameter
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: Agentic CLI error: litellm.NotFoundError: AzureException NotFoundError - The API deployment for this resource does not exist. Or: Could not find model name azure/<name> in litellm model list
rootCause: Azure OpenAI deployment name is different from the model name. The agentic CLI expects them to match
solution: Create a new Azure OpenAI deployment where the deployment name matches the model name exactly (e.g., deployment name gpt-4o for model gpt-4o)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: Agentic CLI error: litellm.llms.azure.common_utils.AzureOpenAIError: Error code 400 - Unsupported value: temperature does not support 1E-8
rootCause: Default temperature parameter value (1E-8) is not supported by the model/deployment
solution: Set environment variable TEMPERATURE=1: export TEMPERATURE=1
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: Agentic CLI error on Windows: ImportError: DLL load failed while importing win32file
rootCause: Corrupted or incompatible Azure CLI installation on Windows, missing win32file DLL dependency
solution: Uninstall and reinstall the latest Azure CLI client
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: Agentic CLI error: litellm.AuthenticationError: AzureException AuthenticationError - Access denied due to invalid subscription key
rootCause: Incorrect or conflicting Azure OpenAI configuration. The tool checks flags first, then config file, then environment variables. A mismatch causes authentication failure
solution: Verify the Azure OpenAI configuration: 1) Check config file (aksAgent.yaml) for correct API key and endpoint. 2) Check environment variables. 3) Check command-line flags. Priority order: flags > config file > environment variables
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentic CLI for AKS

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with OperationNotAllowed: cannot rollback with blue-green upgrade strategy when there is no in-progress blue-green upgrade
rootCause: UpgradeStrategy_BlueGreen requires an active blue/green cycle. If the upgrade already finished or was never started, rollbacks are blocked.
solution: Resume the original blue/green upgrade or switch the pool to rolling strategy, then issue the rollback.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackNoHistory: rollback to node image X with orchestrator Y is not permitted
rootCause: recentlyUsedVersions is empty, usually because this pool has never completed a full upgrade.
solution: Let at least one upgrade finish so history is populated, then retry the rollback.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackVersionNotAllowed: invalid version pair rollback
rootCause: The (nodeImageVersion, orchestratorVersion) pair requested does not match any entry in recentlyUsedVersions.
solution: Fetch the pool (expand properties.recentlyUsedVersions) and choose an exact pair from that list. Alternatively perform another upgrade to create the pair you need.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackVersionExpired - rollback target version pair is older than 7 days
rootCause: The rollback target version pair exists but its timestamp is older than 7 days. AKS refuses rollback for safety after this window.
solution: Roll back to a more recent pair within 7 days, or re-upgrade to refresh history then roll back promptly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: AKS node pool rollback fails with AgentPoolRollbackVersionNotAllowed: Agent pool rollback version is older than control plane by more than 3 minor versions
rootCause: The requested rollback version is more than 3 minors behind the control plane (futureMCVer - futAPVer > 3).
solution: Pick a rollback target within 3 minor versions of the control-plane version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Agentpool Rollback

symptom: AKS Artifact Streaming: Conversion operation failed due to an unknown error when converting image in ACR
rootCause: Artifact Streaming image conversion failed in ACR backend. Root cause needs investigation via ACR Kusto logs.
solution: Query ACR Kusto (cluster ACR, database acrprod, table KubernetesContainers) filtering by operationId shown in CLI output. Check if ACR Artifact Streaming is properly enabled on the repo.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: AKS pod deployment fails with rpc error 503 Service Unavailable when pulling image with Artifact Streaming enabled
rootCause: ACR is not properly attached to AKS, so AKS lacks permission to access ACR for Artifact Streaming.
solution: Verify ACR is attached to AKS properly. Ensure the AKS managed identity has AcrPull role on the ACR. Use az aks check-acr to validate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: Cannot disable Artifact Streaming on an AKS node pool - az aks nodepool update does not expose a way to turn it off
rootCause: AKS does not expose a way to disable Artifact Streaming at the nodepool level via az aks nodepool update.
solution: Delete and recreate the node pool without --enable-artifact-streaming. This is disruptive: nodes will be replaced and workloads rescheduled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: AKS Teleport (private preview) retirement: after April 15 2025 cannot create new node pool with Teleport; after July 15 2025 all Teleport code removed causing potential workload breakage
rootCause: Teleport (private preview) is retired. AKS released Artifact Streaming as replacement in Nov 2023 public preview.
solution: Disable Teleport on existing node pools: az aks nodepool update --aks-custom-headers EnableACRTeleport=false. Migrate to Artifact Streaming: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/aks/artifact-streaming
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Artifact Streaming

symptom: AKS Auto Assign Host Ports: pod mutation skipped with Invalid host port annotation or Unknown protocol in host port annotation in ccp-webhook logs
rootCause: The host port annotation format is invalid. Valid format: <port>/<protocol>,<port>-<port>/<protocol> (e.g. 80/tcp,53/udp,40000-50000/tcp).
solution: Fix the annotation value to match the valid format. Check ccp-webhook mutator logs via Kusto ControlPlaneEventsAll filtering AutoAssignedHostPortPodMutator.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Auto Assign Host Ports

symptom: AKS Auto Assign Host Ports: Failed to allocate host port for container - no enough host ports available or fetch port status error
rootCause: ccp-webhook could not find available ports. Either it failed to fetch pod.HostPort/svc.NodePort status, or all ports in the defined range are occupied.
solution: Check error field in mutator logs. If no enough host ports available: reduce pods per node or expand port range. If fetch port status error: investigate Kubernetes API connectivity from ccp-webhook.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Auto Assign Host Ports

symptom: AKS Workload Identity: pod cannot fetch token, logs show failed to create confidential client with Authority does not appear to use https
rootCause: Workload Identity webhook was not ready when the pod spawned, so AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST and other required env vars were not injected into pod manifest.
solution: Restart the affected pod. The webhook should be ready on subsequent pod creation. Verify webhook running: kubectl logs -n kube-system -l azure-workload-identity.io/system
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure AD Workload Identity

symptom: AKS Workload Identity components running in legacy namespace azure-workload-identity-system instead of kube-system
rootCause: Previous versions of Workload Identity feature used the azure-workload-identity-system namespace. Cluster was not migrated after upgrade.
solution: An IcM to PG is required to perform the migration to kube-system namespace. Query AsyncContextActivity for 'cluster is using legacy webhook namespace' to identify affected clusters.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure AD Workload Identity

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: cilium monitor shows CT: Map insertion failed - connection tracking table filling up causing packet drops
rootCause: Connection tracking (CT) table is filling up due to too many connections within a 5-minute period from pods on the node.
solution: Schedule fewer pods per node, or investigate if application workload is creating excessive connections. NOTE: Cilium docs suggest adjusting CT GC interval or map size, but this is NOT currently configurable in AKS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: cluster has multiple nodes but only one cilium-operator pod running instead of expected two
rootCause: Customer likely created cluster with single node then scaled up. Cilium operator replica count was not reconciled.
solution: Reconcile the cluster (e.g. az aks update or trigger a no-op operation). After reconcile, should see two cilium-operator replicas.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: single node cluster has two cilium-operator pods with one stuck in Pending state
rootCause: Customer created cluster with multiple nodes then scaled down to single node. Cilium operator still expects 2 replicas.
solution: Reconcile the cluster to adjust cilium-operator replica count to 1 for single-node cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: ASI shows Cluster Degraded with CustomerNodesNotReadyCNI even though nodes are actually Ready
rootCause: Known display issue in ASI. Cilium CNI is installed via daemonset at node startup and ASI detects the brief initial NotReady state, showing Degraded even after CNI installation completes.
solution: Verify actual node status in ASI Node Pools tab. If nodes show Ready, cluster is healthy - this is an ASI display issue. Fix expected in the new monitoring stack.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: Unattended Upgrades on Ubuntu 22.04 leaves nodes in NotReady state until manually restarted
rootCause: Known issue affecting both BYO CNI + Cilium and AKS-managed Cilium on Ubuntu 22.04. Unattended OS upgrades break Cilium networking.
solution: Manually mitigate by restarting affected nodes. Track repair: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/CloudNativeCompute/_workitems/edit/17508542
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS Cilium CNI: aad-pod-identity pods experience i/o timeout connecting to 169.254.169.254 (IMDS) on Cilium clusters
rootCause: Known bug with aad-pod-identity on Cilium clusters. aad-pod-identity is deprecated since Oct 2023.
solution: Migrate to Azure Workload Identity: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/workload-identity-overview. aad-pod-identity is no longer maintained.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Cilium CNI

symptom: AKS Azure Linux: container fails with certificate errors because /etc/ssl/certs is a symbolic link to /etc/pki/tls/certs which does not resolve inside the container
rootCause: Azure Linux (RPM-based) stores certificates at /etc/pki/tls/certs with /etc/ssl/certs as a symlink. Containers mapping only /etc/ssl/certs get a dangling symlink since /etc/pki is not mapped.
solution: Map /etc/pki into the container using a hostPath volume with type DirectoryOrCreate. This works on both Ubuntu and Azure Linux hosts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Linux (Mariner)

symptom: AKS Azure Linux: OSSKU is invalid or InvalidOSSKU error when creating or upgrading node pool with AzureLinux3 osSku on unsupported Kubernetes version
rootCause: The osSku AzureLinux3 is not supported for the Kubernetes version being used, or required feature flags (AFEC) are not registered. CVM on AzureLinux requires Azure Linux 3.0+.
solution: Check supported osSku by k8s version: AzureLinux3 GA on k8s 1.28-1.36. AzureLinux2 retirement Nov 30 2025. For CVM: requires Azure Linux 3.0+ (k8s 1.28+). Register AFEC flag if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Azure Linux (Mariner)

symptom: KMS etcd encryption fails with StatusCode=403 ForbiddenByFirewall: 'Client address is not authorized and caller is not a trusted service' when KMS plugin tries to encrypt/decrypt secrets via Azure Key Vault
rootCause: Azure Key Vault has firewall enabled, blocking the KMS plugin (running on AKS control plane) from accessing the vault endpoint
solution: Disable the firewall on Azure Key Vault to allow KMS plugin access. Alternatively, if using private key vault, enable API Server VNet Integration (az aks update --enable-apiserver-vnet-integration) and add the API server subnet to Key Vault allowed networks
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/KMS etcd Encryption

symptom: KMS etcd encryption fails with StatusCode=403 ForbiddenByPolicy: AKS managed identity does not have keys encrypt permission on key vault
rootCause: Key Vault access policy is missing Encrypt and Decrypt key permissions for the AKS cluster managed identity (or the permissions were deleted)
solution: Assign Key Vault access policy with Encrypt and Decrypt key permissions for the AKS managed identity. Follow https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/assign-access-policy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/KMS etcd Encryption

symptom: KMS deadlock: AKS cluster with KMS private keyvault + konnectivity gets stuck after stop/start; apiserver cannot start because KMS plugin cannot reach private Key Vault while konnectivity is down
rootCause: Circular dependency: konnectivity requires apiserver, apiserver requires KMS to decrypt etcd, KMS requires konnectivity to reach private Key Vault. During cluster stop/start konnectivity shuts down breaking the loop. Private keyvault + konnectivity support ended Aug 22 2025
solution: Enable API Server VNet Integration: az aks update --enable-apiserver-vnet-integration. This removes the konnectivity dependency for KMS access to private Key Vault. For public key vaults this issue does not occur
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/KMS etcd Encryption

symptom: KMS plugin logs show 'using system-assigned managed identity to retrieve access token' causing confusion about whether system-assigned MI is supported for KMS
rootCause: KMS only supports user-assigned managed identity and service principal, NOT system-assigned MI. The log is misleading: msi-adapter internally uses system-assigned MI to provide user-assigned MI tokens to KMS plugin, which is by design
solution: This log is expected behavior. Ensure the cluster is configured with a user-assigned managed identity (not system-assigned) for KMS. The msi-adapter component handles the token bridge internally
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/KMS etcd Encryption

symptom: Image Integrity policy reports 'System error calling external data provider' in Azure Policy compliance results
rootCause: Ratify pod (gatekeeper-system/ratify) does not exist or is unhealthy on the cluster; Image Integrity addon may not be properly enabled
solution: 1) Check Image Integrity addon enabled via ASI. If not, customer may be using OSS ratify (out of support). 2) If enabled, check ratify pod health: kubectl get pods -n gatekeeper-system. 3) Check logs if unhealthy. 4) If unresolved raise IcM to AKS/Security
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Image Integrity

symptom: Image Integrity policy reports 'failed notation verification' for container images that customer believes are properly signed with notation
rootCause: The verifier and/or certificateStore CRDs are not configured correctly; the certificateStore does not contain the correct signing certificate (CA) for the image notation signature
solution: 1) Verify the image is actually signed with notation. 2) Check verifier CRD configuration. 3) Ensure certificateStore has correct signing cert (CA) info and status shows success: kubectl get certificateStore -o yaml
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Image Integrity

symptom: Image Integrity policy reports 'signature artifact not found' for container images, deployment denied or marked non-compliant
rootCause: The container image has not been signed with notation; no signature artifact exists in the registry for this image
solution: Customer must sign the container image using notation before deploying. As a temporary mitigation change the policy effect from Deny to Audit to unblock deployments
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Image Integrity

symptom: Attempting to change GPU driver installation mode on existing AKS nodepool fails with error: Changing property 'properties.gpuProfile.installGPUDriver' is not allowed
rootCause: GPU driver installation mode (Install/None) is immutable once configured on a nodepool; AKS does not support changing this property after nodepool creation
solution: Delete and recreate the nodepool with the desired GPU driver installation mode. There is no way to change this in-place
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Linux GPU

symptom: ErrorCode_InvalidCustomKubeletConfig on Windows nodepool: cpuManagerPolicy, cpuCfsQuota, cpuCfsQuotaPeriod, topologyManagerPolicy, allowedUnsafeSysctls, or podMaxPids is not supported by Windows. Also CustomLinuxOSConfig cannot be set for Windows agent pools
rootCause: By design - these kubelet config parameters and Linux OS config are not supported on Windows node pools in AKS
solution: Remove unsupported kubelet config parameters (cpuManagerPolicy, cpuCfsQuota, cpuCfsQuotaPeriod, topologyManagerPolicy, allowedUnsafeSysctls, podMaxPids) for Windows node pools. Do not set CustomLinuxOSConfig for Windows agent pools
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Custom Node Config

symptom: ErrorCode_CustomKubeletConfigOrCustomLinuxOSConfigCanNotBeChanged: CustomKubeletConfig or CustomLinuxOSConfig can not be changed for this operation when trying to update nodepool or cluster
rootCause: By design - CustomKubeletConfig and CustomLinuxOSConfig can only be set during initial node pool creation, not during update operations
solution: Create a new node pool with the desired CustomKubeletConfig/CustomLinuxOSConfig settings. These cannot be modified after creation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Custom Node Config

symptom: Container logs do not get rotated upon hitting --container-log-max-size on Windows nodes. Log files grow unbounded despite kubelet log rotation configuration
rootCause: Kubelet log compression bug on Windows in k8s versions prior to v1.23.13, v1.24.7, and v1.25.0. Fixed in kubernetes/kubernetes#111548. Even after patching, heavy log writes may still cause rotation delays (upstream issue #110630)
solution: Upgrade k8s version to v1.23.15+ or v1.24.9+ or v1.25.0+. If logs still do not rotate after upgrade during heavy writes: restart containers, use higher disk I/O throughput VMs, or redirect logs to persistent volume
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Custom Node Config

symptom: Delete Machines API returns NotFound with resultSubCode GetAgentPool_NotFound: Could not find the agentpool in subscription/resourceGroup/resourceName
rootCause: Agentpool is not registered in DB yet, or the target URI does not have the correct agentpool name as path parameter
solution: Verify the agentpool exists by checking List AgentPools API response or GET Agentpool. Ensure the correct agentpool name is used in the request URI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Delete Specific Node Machine

symptom: Delete Machines API returns InvalidParameter: Cannot find any valid machines to delete. Please check your input machine names
rootCause: Customer provided invalid machine names in the DELETE request body. Machine names must match existing VMSS instances in the agent pool
solution: Run az aks machine list to get valid machine names. Provide correct names in request body: {machineNames: [aks-agentpool0-xxxxx-vmss000001]}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Delete Specific Node Machine

symptom: Deployment Safeguards (Guardrails) enable fails with errors: Invalid K8s version, Azure policy not enabled, GuardrailsPreview feature not registered, invalid version, or Azure Policy checkaccess not allowed (missing permissions)
rootCause: Multiple prerequisites not met: K8s >= 1.19, Azure Policy Add-On enabled, GuardrailsPreview feature flag registered, and Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/read+write permissions. Contributor role is NOT sufficient for the write permission
solution: Ensure all prerequisites: 1) upgrade cluster to k8s >= 1.19, 2) enable Azure Policy Add-On, 3) register Microsoft.ContainerService/GuardrailsPreview feature flag, 4) grant Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/write permission (beyond Contributor). AKS RP calls Azure Policy OBO customer so both need permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Deployment Safeguards (Azure Policy)

symptom: Deployment Safeguards (Guardrails) policy assignment does not appear on cluster after enabling. Customer cannot see guardrails-policy-assignment
rootCause: Expected behavior - Azure Policy assignment propagation to the AKS cluster can take up to 75 minutes after initial creation
solution: Wait up to 75 minutes for the assignment to appear. Verify via: az policy assignment show --scope <MC_resource_id> -n guardrails-policy-assignment. Check constraint download errors via GuardrailsContextActivity Kusto table. Escalate constraint issues to Azure Policy team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Deployment Safeguards (Azure Policy)

symptom: Customer wants to disable CSI storage drivers and use in-tree drivers, or needs to troubleshoot CSI driver enable/disable operations on AKS cluster
solution: Use CLI flags: --disable-disk-driver / --disable-file-driver / --disable-snapshot-controller (and --enable-* to re-enable). Check status in ASI Feature section. Troubleshoot: check kube-addon-manager logs, OverlayMgrEvents, Frontend/Async Kusto tables. Escalate to PG for unresolvable issues
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Disable CSI Drivers

symptom: Dual-stack (IPv4/IPv6) kubenet cluster cannot scale beyond 200 nodes. Node additions fail or route table limit exceeded
rootCause: Azure route table limit is 400 routes. In dual-stack kubenet, each node requires 2 routes (one for IPv4, one for IPv6), limiting the cluster to 200 nodes maximum
solution: This is a platform limitation. For >200 nodes with dual-stack, migrate to CNI Overlay dual-stack (no route tables). For kubenet dual-stack, stay within 200-node limit. Also note: LoadBalancer services do not support ExternalTrafficPolicy:Cluster for Mariner nodes in dual-stack
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Dual-Stack IPv6

symptom: 401 Unauthorized errors when authenticating to AKS API server using external identity provider (GitHub OIDC, Google OAuth). Token validation fails at API server
rootCause: Token audience (aud claim) mismatch with JWT authenticator config, or issuer URL does not exactly match iss claim (including protocol/trailing slashes), or identity provider OAuth/OIDC settings are incorrect
solution: 1) Verify audience claim in token matches JWT authenticator config. 2) Correct issuer URL to exactly match iss claim. 3) For GitHub: ensure workflow has id-token:write permission and audience matches. 4) For Google: verify OAuth 2.0 client ID/secret. Update: az aks jwtauthenticator update --config-file updated-jwt-config.json
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/External Identity Providers for AKS Control Plane

symptom: AKS external identity provider authentication fails: username/groups not recognized or conflicts. Missing required aks:jwt: prefix in claim mappings or invalid CEL expressions
rootCause: JWT authenticator claim mappings missing aks:jwt: prefix (required to prevent conflicts with other auth methods), or CEL expressions have syntax errors or return unexpected data types
solution: Update JWT authenticator config: 1) Add aks:jwt: prefix to all username/groups mappings (e.g. username: aks:jwt:github: + claims.sub). 2) Test CEL expressions with evaluator for correct return types (string for username, array of strings for groups). 3) Add userValidationRules to block system-prefixed usernames
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/External Identity Providers for AKS Control Plane

symptom: AKS cluster minor version upgrade fails with UpgradeBlockedOnDeprecatedAPIUsage: Control Plane upgrade is blocked due to recent usage of a Kubernetes API deprecated in the specified version
rootCause: AKS fail-fast feature detects deprecated k8s API usage within last 1-12 hours for the target version. Applies to minor version MC upgrades, k8s >= 1.26, preview API >= 2023-01-02-preview. Detection via CCP prometheus + remediator recording in configmap
solution: Option 1: Remove deprecated API usage and wait 12 hours for records to expire. Option 2: Set upgradeSettings.overrideSettings with controlPlaneOverrides=[IgnoreKubernetesDeprecations] and until timestamp to bypass. Find usage: query kube-audit logs (AzureDiagnostics where Category == kube-audit and log_s has deprecated). Internal: CPRemediatorLogs or kubectl get configmap cluster-upgrade-configmap -n <ccpNamespace>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Fail Fast Upgrade on Breaking API Changes

symptom: InvalidOSSKU error when creating AKS cluster/nodepool with osSku=Flatcar: Subscription needs to register AKSFlatcarPreview feature flag
rootCause: Flatcar Container Linux for AKS is in preview and requires explicit feature flag registration on the subscription
solution: Register the AKSFlatcarPreview feature flag: az feature register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService --name AKSFlatcarPreview. Wait for propagation then retry
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Flatcar Container Linux for AKS

symptom: Flatcar nodepool creation fails with InvalidOSSKU/CVMIncompatibleWithConfig/BadRequest when combined with unsupported features: FIPS, SecurityPatch channel, Gen1 VMs, CVM, Artifact Streaming, Trusted Launch, or Pod Sandboxing
rootCause: Flatcar Container Linux has specific limitations: no FIPS, no SecurityPatch channel (only NodeImage/None), Gen2 VMs only, no CVM, no Artifact Streaming, no Trusted Launch, no Pod Sandboxing, no GPU workloads
solution: Remove the incompatible feature. Use Gen2 VM sizes. Use NodeImage or None upgrade channels. GPU workloads blocked pending AgentBaker support. Create new nodepools with Flatcar; OS migration to Flatcar is not supported
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Flatcar Container Linux for AKS

symptom: InvalidOSSKU when trying to change existing nodepool OSSKU to Flatcar: Only allowed to change OSSKU to Ubuntu, AzureLinux, Ubuntu2404, Ubuntu2204, AzureLinux3
rootCause: OS migration to Flatcar is not currently supported. OSSKU change only allows migration between Ubuntu and AzureLinux variants
solution: Create a new node pool with osSku=Flatcar. Cordon/drain the old nodepool, move workloads, then delete the old nodepool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Flatcar Container Linux for AKS

symptom: Host Network NSG Control: Reconcile application security group failed error, or customer cannot access through specified host ports on nodepool with allowed-host-ports configured
rootCause: Feature not enabled or misconfigured: allowed-host-ports range does not match port being accessed, NSG rules not created/reconciled, or resource is on nodepool without feature enabled
solution: 1) Verify feature enabled in ASI nodepool page. 2) Check allowed-host-ports matches desired ports. 3) Verify NSG has rules allowing allowed-host-ports with ASG (do not manually modify NSG). 4) Reconcile cluster if NSG rules missing. Update config via CLI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Host Network NSG Control

symptom: Host Network NSG Control: cannot delete managed ASG because there are still references to it from VMSS or NSG. ASG deletion fails with reference error
rootCause: Managed ASG was deleted before VMSS and NSG references were removed
solution: Manually update VMSS and NSG to remove references to the managed ASG, then reconcile the agent pool
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/Platform and Tools/Feature Specific/Host Network NSG Control
